# US-03: "View Logic" Toggle for LLM Reasoning in Observation Detail

## Problem
Carla Navarro is a workspace admin who can now see that observations have reasoning stored (after US-01), but the observation detail page in the web UI does not expose this field. She still has to query the database directly to read reasoning. The "why" exists in the data but is not accessible in her normal workflow.

## Who
- Workspace admin | Investigating Observer findings in the web UI | Needs one-click access to LLM reasoning without leaving the observation detail page

## Solution
Add a "View Logic" toggle button to the observation detail view that expands a panel showing the LLM chain-of-thought reasoning, model identifier, and trace link. The panel handles three states: reasoning available, deterministic fallback (no LLM), and legacy observation (no reasoning field).

## Job Traces
- J1: Forensic Debugging (primary)
- J3: Audit/Compliance (secondary -- reasoning visible during compliance review)

## Domain Examples

### 1: Full Reasoning Available -- Carla investigates contradiction

Carla opens observation "observation:a1b2c3d4" (conflict severity, source: llm). She clicks "View Logic." The panel expands showing:
- Full reasoning text referencing task:i9j0k1l2 and decision:e5f6g7h8
- Model: "anthropic/claude-sonnet" (from linked trace)
- Trace link: "trace:x1y2z3" (clickable to view token count, cost, latency)

She reads the reasoning, confirms it is sound, and clicks "Acknowledge."

### 2: Deterministic Fallback -- No LLM reasoning exists

Carla opens observation "observation:det001" (info severity, source: deterministic_fallback). She clicks "View Logic." The panel shows:
- "Reasoning unavailable: verification used deterministic fallback (LLM call failed or was skipped)"
- Deterministic verdict: "match"
- Source: "CI status check -- all checks passing"

She understands why no LLM reasoning exists and moves on.

### 3: Legacy Observation -- Pre-feature observation

Carla opens observation "observation:old001" created 3 months ago (before this feature). She clicks "View Logic." The panel shows:
- "No reasoning recorded for this observation. Observations created before March 2026 do not include LLM chain-of-thought."
- Trace link shown as fallback if source_session linkage exists

## UAT Scenarios (BDD)

### Scenario: View Logic shows full reasoning for LLM-sourced observation
Given observation "observation:a1b2c3d4" has reasoning "The task Migrate billing..." and source "llm"
And the observation is linked to a trace via source_session
When Carla clicks "View Logic" on the observation detail page
Then a panel expands below the observation text
And the panel displays the full reasoning text
And the panel shows the model identifier from the linked trace
And the panel shows a clickable trace link

### Scenario: View Logic shows fallback for deterministic-sourced observation
Given observation "observation:det001" has no reasoning and source "deterministic_fallback"
When Carla clicks "View Logic"
Then the panel shows "Reasoning unavailable: verification used deterministic fallback"
And the deterministic verdict is displayed

### Scenario: View Logic shows empty state for legacy observation
Given observation "observation:old001" has no reasoning field (NONE)
And the observation was created before the reasoning feature
When Carla clicks "View Logic"
Then the panel shows "No reasoning recorded for this observation"
And a trace link is shown as fallback if available

### Scenario: View Logic toggle hides reasoning on second click
Given Carla has clicked "View Logic" and the reasoning panel is visible
When Carla clicks "Hide Logic"
Then the reasoning panel collapses
And the observation detail returns to its default compact view

### Scenario: Reasoning is not shown by default (internal telemetry)
Given Carla opens observation "observation:a1b2c3d4" with reasoning populated
When the observation detail page loads
Then the reasoning panel is collapsed by default
And only the observation text, severity, confidence, and evidence_refs are visible
And the "View Logic" button is visible as a toggle

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] "View Logic" toggle button visible on observation detail when user is workspace admin
- [ ] Reasoning panel collapsed by default (internal telemetry, not primary content)
- [ ] Panel shows full reasoning text when source is "llm" or "peer_review"
- [ ] Panel shows fallback message when source is "deterministic_fallback"
- [ ] Panel shows empty state message when reasoning is NONE (legacy observations)
- [ ] Panel shows model identifier and trace link when trace is available
- [ ] Panel handles missing trace gracefully

## Technical Notes
- Observation detail API endpoint needs to include `reasoning` field in response for admin users only
- Access control: reasoning field should be excluded from non-admin API responses (internal telemetry)
- Trace linkage: observation -> source_session -> invoked -> trace. May require an additional query or eager load.
- UI component: collapsible panel with monospace text formatting for reasoning (similar to code block)
- No new API endpoints needed -- extend existing observation detail response

## Dependencies
- US-01 (reasoning field must exist on observation records)

input.content:
# US-04: Observer Reasoning Queries for Drift Detection

## Problem
The Observer agent currently detects behavioral drift through output patterns only -- observation severity distribution, confidence histograms, false positive rates. It cannot audit the QUALITY of its own reasoning because reasoning is not persisted. If the LLM starts producing shorter, less evidence-grounded verdicts, this degradation is invisible until false positives pile up and a human notices.

## Who
- Observer agent | Self-calibrating reasoning quality during graph scans | Needs to query recent reasoning programmatically
- Behavior scorer | Evaluating Observer agent quality | Needs reasoning content as input to scoring functions

## Solution
Provide query functions that load observation reasoning for programmatic analysis. The Observer can use these during self-calibration, and the behavior scorer can use them during evaluation cycles.

## Job Traces
- J2: Drift Detection (primary)

## Domain Examples

### 1: Observer Loads Recent Reasoning for Self-Calibration -- Elena's workspace

The Observer agent runs a scheduled scan on workspace "TechCorp Platform" managed by Elena Rodriguez. Before processing new entities, the Observer loads the last 50 observations with reasoning to calibrate its baseline:

Query returns 47 observations with reasoning (3 were deterministic fallback). The Observer computes:
- Average reasoning length: 312 characters (down from 428 last month)
- Average evidence references in reasoning: 1.8 (down from 2.4)
- Confidence distribution: 72% clustered between 0.70-0.75 (was evenly distributed 0.65-0.90)

The Observer creates an observation: "Verification reasoning quality showing signs of degradation: reasoning length down 27%, evidence density down 25%, confidence clustering near threshold."

### 2: Behavior Scorer Evaluates Observer -- Monthly scoring cycle

The behavior scorer runs the "Observer Verification Quality" behavior definition for workspace "Acme Product." It loads 30 recent observations with reasoning and evaluates:
- Specificity: 80% of reasoning texts reference specific entity IDs (passing threshold: 70%)
- Evidence grounding: 65% cite 2+ evidence refs (below threshold: 75%) -- score: 0.72
- Trend: down from 0.81 last period

The scorer records the trend and the behavior definition's remediation guidance triggers: "Consider reviewing Observer system prompt for evidence grounding emphasis."

### 3: No Reasoning Available -- New workspace with few observations

Workspace "StartupMVP" has only 5 observations, all from deterministic fallback (no LLM verification was triggered). The reasoning query returns 0 results. The Observer skips self-calibration and the behavior scorer records "insufficient data" for this period.

## UAT Scenarios (BDD)

### Scenario: Observer queries observations with reasoning for drift analysis
Given workspace "TechCorp Platform" has 50 observations created in the last 30 days
And 47 have reasoning populated (source: "llm" or "peer_review")
And 3 have no reasoning (source: "deterministic_fallback")
When the Observer queries observations with reasoning for self-calibration
Then the query returns 47 observations with reasoning, confidence, source, and created_at
And observations without reasoning are excluded from the result set
And results are ordered by created_at descending

### Scenario: Behavior scorer loads reasoning for quality evaluation
Given behavior definition "Observer Verification Quality" targets the Observer agent
And workspace "Acme Product" has 30 recent observations with reasoning
When the behavior scorer requests reasoning for evaluation
Then the scorer receives reasoning text and evidence_refs for each observation
And the scorer can compute specificity and evidence grounding metrics

### Scenario: Empty result when no LLM-backed observations exist
Given workspace "StartupMVP" has 5 observations all with source "deterministic_fallback"
When the Observer queries observations with reasoning
Then the query returns an empty result set
And the Observer skips self-calibration gracefully

### Scenario: Reasoning query respects workspace scope
Given observations with reasoning exist in both "TechCorp Platform" and "Acme Product"
When the Observer queries reasoning for workspace "TechCorp Platform"
Then only observations from "TechCorp Platform" are returned
And no cross-workspace reasoning leakage occurs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Query function returns observations with reasoning, confidence, source, evidence_refs, and created_at
- [ ] Query filters out observations where reasoning is NONE
- [ ] Query respects workspace scope
- [ ] Query supports configurable limit and time range
- [ ] Observer can consume query results for self-calibration metrics
- [ ] Behavior scorer can consume query results for scoring input

## Technical Notes
- New query function in `observation/queries.ts`: `listObservationsWithReasoning(input: { surreal, workspaceRecord, limit, sinceDate? })`
- SurrealQL: `SELECT id, reasoning, confidence, source, evidence_refs, created_at FROM observation WHERE workspace = $workspace AND reasoning != NONE ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT $limit`
- Per SurrealDB conventions: use bound parameters, RecordId for workspace
- This function is used by Observer agent context building (agents/observer/) and behavior scorer (behavior/)
- No new API endpoint needed -- this is internal server-side query only

## Dependencies
- US-01 (reasoning field must exist on observations for the query to return data)

# Definition of Ready Validation: LLM Reasoning Telemetry Stories

## US-01: Persist LLM Reasoning on Observations

| DoR Item | Status | Evidence |
|----------|--------|----------|
| Problem statement clear, domain language | PASS | "Carla Navarro investigates Observer findings... finds it impossible to determine WHY... wastes 30-60 minutes per investigation" |
| User/persona with specific characteristics | PASS | Carla Navarro, workspace admin, 3 active projects, reviews findings daily. Observer agent (programmatic consumer). Behavior scorer (programmatic consumer). |
| 3+ domain examples with real data | PASS | (1) Verification verdict reasoning for billing-tRPC contradiction, (2) Peer review reasoning for Redis caching observation, (3) Anomaly evaluation reasoning for GDPR docs task |
| UAT scenarios in Given/When/Then (3-7) | PASS | 5 scenarios: verification stored, peer review stored, deterministic fallback, LLM failure, contradiction detection |
| AC derived from UAT | PASS | 5 AC items map to the 5 scenarios |
| Right-sized (1-3 days, 3-7 scenarios) | PASS | ~2 days: 1 schema migration + 4 pipeline integration points + createObservation() update. 5 scenarios. |
| Technical notes identify constraints | PASS | Schema migration path, 4 LLM reasoning paths enumerated, option<string> convention noted |
| Dependencies resolved or tracked | PASS | No external dependencies. Self-contained. |

**DoR Status: PASSED**

---

## US-02: Persist LLM Reasoning on Intent Authorization

| DoR Item | Status | Evidence |
|----------|--------|----------|
| Problem statement clear, domain language | PASS | "evaluation.reason shows one-line summary... cannot see full chain-of-thought... making compliance review shallow" |
| User/persona with specific characteristics | PASS | Carla Navarro auditing intent authorization decisions. Compliance reviewer role described. |
| 3+ domain examples with real data | PASS | (1) Deploy billing service with budget reasoning, (2) Policy-only config update (no LLM), (3) Rejected delete-production-records with denial reasoning |
| UAT scenarios in Given/When/Then (3-7) | PASS | 4 scenarios: LLM reasoning stored, policy-only no reasoning, rejected intent reasoning, admin views both fields |
| AC derived from UAT | PASS | 5 AC items covering field addition, pipeline integration, field distinction, policy-only handling |
| Right-sized (1-3 days, 3-7 scenarios) | PASS | ~1.5 days: 1 schema migration + 1 pipeline integration point. 4 scenarios. |
| Technical notes identify constraints | PASS | Schema migration, intent-submission.ts path, naming rationale (llm_reasoning vs reasoning), option<string> |
| Dependencies resolved or tracked | PASS | Depends on US-01 for pattern establishment (soft dependency, not blocking) |

**DoR Status: PASSED**

---

## US-03: "View Logic" Toggle in Observation Detail UI

| DoR Item | Status | Evidence |
|----------|--------|----------|
| Problem statement clear, domain language | PASS | "reasoning exists in data but is not accessible in her normal workflow" |
| User/persona with specific characteristics | PASS | Carla Navarro, workspace admin, web UI workflow |
| 3+ domain examples with real data | PASS | (1) Full reasoning with trace link for contradiction, (2) Deterministic fallback display, (3) Legacy observation empty state |
| UAT scenarios in Given/When/Then (3-7) | PASS | 5 scenarios: full reasoning, fallback, legacy, toggle hide, default collapsed |
| AC derived from UAT | PASS | 7 AC items covering toggle visibility, panel states, model/trace display, access control |
| Right-sized (1-3 days, 3-7 scenarios) | PASS | ~2 days: UI component + API response extension + access control. 5 scenarios. |
| Technical notes identify constraints | PASS | API response extension, access control note, trace linkage query, UI component pattern |
| Dependencies resolved or tracked | PASS | Depends on US-01 (hard dependency -- field must exist) |

**DoR Status: PASSED**

---

## US-04: Observer Reasoning Queries for Drift Detection

| DoR Item | Status | Evidence |
|----------|--------|----------|
| Problem statement clear, domain language | PASS | "cannot audit QUALITY of its own reasoning... degradation is invisible until false positives pile up" |
| User/persona with specific characteristics | PASS | Observer agent (self-calibration), Behavior scorer (evaluation cycle) |
| 3+ domain examples with real data | PASS | (1) Elena's workspace self-calibration with metrics, (2) Monthly behavior scoring with threshold evaluation, (3) New workspace with insufficient data |
| UAT scenarios in Given/When/Then (3-7) | PASS | 4 scenarios: reasoning query, scorer load, empty result, workspace scope |
| AC derived from UAT | PASS | 6 AC items covering query function, filtering, scope, limits, consumer compatibility |
| Right-sized (1-3 days, 3-7 scenarios) | PASS | ~1 day: single query function + caller integration. 4 scenarios. |
| Technical notes identify constraints | PASS | Query function signature, SurrealQL, no new API endpoint, consumer paths listed |
| Dependencies resolved or tracked | PASS | Depends on US-01 (hard dependency -- field must have data) |

**DoR Status: PASSED**

---

## Summary

| Story | DoR Status | Effort Estimate | Priority (MoSCoW) |
|-------|-----------|----------------|-------------------|
| US-01: Persist Reasoning on Observations | PASSED | 2 days | Must Have |
| US-02: Persist Reasoning on Intents | PASSED | 1.5 days | Should Have |
| US-03: View Logic UI Toggle | PASSED | 2 days | Should Have |
| US-04: Observer Reasoning Queries | PASSED | 1 day | Should Have |

### Implementation Order
1. **US-01** first (schema + pipeline -- enables all other stories)
2. **US-02** second (schema + pipeline -- parallel with US-03 if desired)
3. **US-03** and **US-04** can proceed in parallel after US-01

### Total Estimated Effort
~6.5 days for full feature across schema, backend, and UI layers.

### Risk Assessment

| Risk | Probability | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|-------------|--------|------------|
| Reasoning text is very long for some LLM calls (token-heavy prompts) | Medium | Low | Reasoning is the LLM OUTPUT (chain-of-thought), not the input prompt. Output is typically 100-500 chars. No truncation needed. |
| Legacy observations cause UI confusion (no reasoning) | Low | Medium | US-03 handles three states explicitly: reasoning available, fallback, legacy empty state |
| Access control gap exposes reasoning to non-admin agents | Medium | Medium | US-03 AC includes access control requirement. Reasoning excluded from non-admin API responses. |
| Observer self-calibration creates noise observations | Low | Low | Drift observations should be info severity, not conflict. Behavior scorer is the primary drift consumer. |

1→# JTBD Analysis: Store LLM Reasoning as Internal Telemetry
     2→
     3→## Context
     4→
     5→LLM reasoning (chain of thought) produced by the Observer verification pipeline, peer review, graph scan anomaly evaluation, contradiction detection, and learning diagnosis is currently used at runtime but discarded after execution. This reasoning is the justification trail for every autonomous agent action -- critical for forensics, auditing, and drift detection.
     6→
     7→### Current State
     8→
     9→- `generateVerificationVerdict()` returns `LlmVerdict` with `reasoning` field -- used in `applyLlmVerdict()` to set `VerificationResult.text`, but the original structured reasoning is not persisted on the observation.
    10→- `generatePeerReviewVerdict()` returns `PeerReviewVerdict` with `reasoning` -- logged but not stored.
    11→- Graph scan anomaly evaluation returns `reasoning` per entity -- logged at `observer.scan.llm_filtered` but not persisted.
    12→- Contradiction detection returns `reasoning` per contradiction pair -- used in observation text but chain-of-thought lost.
    13→- Learning diagnosis returns `rootCauseSchema` with `reasoning` -- used but not stored separately.
    14→- The `observation` table has NO `reasoning` field.
    15→- The `intent` table HAS a `reasoning` field but it stores human-provided rationale, not LLM chain-of-thought.
    16→- The `trace` table captures model stats (tokens, cost, latency) per LLM call but NOT the chain-of-thought content.
    17→
    18→### Design Constraint
    19→
    20→`model_stats` is NOT needed on observation or intent -- the existing `trace` table already captures model, input_tokens, output_tokens, cost_usd, latency_ms per trace entry. The feature stores reasoning text only, linking to traces for cost/performance data.
    21→
    22→---
    23→
    24→## Job 1: Forensic Debugging
    25→
    26→### Job Story
    27→
    28→**When** a workspace admin discovers that an agent took an unexpected action (e.g., the Observer flagged a task as contradicting a confirmed decision, and the admin is unsure why),
    29→**I want to** see the LLM's chain-of-thought reasoning that led to that action,
    30→**so I can** quickly determine whether the agent reasoned correctly or hallucinated, and take corrective action within minutes instead of hours.
    31→
    32→### Functional Job
    33→Retrieve and read the exact LLM reasoning behind a specific observation or intent evaluation to diagnose whether the agent's logic was sound.
    34→
    35→### Emotional Job
    36→Feel confident and in control when investigating agent failures -- knowing the full "why" is available, not just the "what."
    37→
    38→### Social Job
    39→Demonstrate to the team that autonomous agents are debuggable and transparent, not black boxes that require blind trust.
    40→
    41→### Four Forces Analysis
    42→
    43→| Force | Description |
    44→|-------|-------------|
    45→| **Push** | When an agent creates a false-positive conflict observation, the admin currently has no way to see WHY the LLM reached that conclusion. They must re-run the scan mentally, guess at the prompt, or read raw logs. This wastes 30-60 minutes per incident. |
    46→| **Pull** | One-click "View Logic" toggle on any observation or intent shows the exact chain-of-thought. Root cause identified in under 2 minutes. The reasoning is right there, next to the observation text. |
    47→| **Anxiety** | "Will storing reasoning bloat the database?" / "Will this expose prompt internals that shouldn't be visible to all users?" / "Will the reasoning be stale or misleading if the underlying data changed?" |
    48→| **Habit** | Currently, admins grep server logs (`observer.llm.call`, `observer.scan.llm_filtered`) to find reasoning. It works but is slow and requires terminal access. |
    49→
    50→### Assessment
    51→- Switch likelihood: **High** -- the push is strong (no current way to see reasoning in-context) and the pull is immediately tangible.
    52→- Key blocker: Anxiety about access control (reasoning is internal telemetry, not for all agents).
    53→- Key enabler: Push from painful log-grepping workflow.
    54→- Design implication: Reasoning must be stored as internal telemetry visible to Observer and workspace admins only. "View Logic" toggle in UI, not shown by default.
    55→
    56→---
    57→
    58→## Job 2: Drift Detection
    59→
    60→### Job Story
    61→
    62→**When** the Observer agent is evaluating whether agent behavior has degraded over time (e.g., verification confidence scores are trending downward, or anomaly evaluations are increasingly being filtered as false positives),
    63→**I want to** audit the reasoning across multiple observations to detect patterns of degraded reasoning quality,
    64→**so I can** catch behavioral drift before it compounds into systemic failures.
    65→
    66→### Functional Job
    67→Query and compare LLM reasoning across a time range of observations to identify reasoning quality degradation patterns.
    68→
    69→### Emotional Job
    70→Feel proactive rather than reactive -- catching drift early rather than discovering it after a cascade of bad decisions.
    71→
    72→### Social Job
    73→Demonstrate to stakeholders that the autonomous system has self-monitoring capabilities -- it does not just act, it audits its own logic.
    74→
    75→### Four Forces Analysis
    76→
    77→| Force | Description |
    78→|-------|-------------|
    79→| **Push** | The Observer currently detects drift through signals (stale tasks, status mismatches) but cannot audit its own reasoning quality. If the LLM starts producing lower-quality verdicts, this is invisible until false positives pile up. The behavior scorer has no access to reasoning content. |
    80→| **Pull** | Reasoning stored on observations enables the behavior scorer to evaluate reasoning quality over time. The Observer can load recent reasoning as context for self-calibration. Trend analysis becomes possible: "In the last 50 observations, reasoning length dropped 40% and confidence scores clustered at 0.71 (just above threshold)." |
    81→| **Anxiety** | "Will programmatic reasoning analysis itself be unreliable?" / "How much additional token cost to analyze stored reasoning?" |
    82→| **Habit** | Currently, drift is detected through output patterns only (observation severity distribution, confidence histograms). Adding reasoning analysis is a new capability, not a replacement. |
    83→
    84→### Assessment
    85→- Switch likelihood: **High** -- this is a new capability that does not exist today. No habit to overcome.
    86→- Key blocker: Anxiety about reliability of meta-reasoning (LLM evaluating LLM reasoning).
    87→- Key enabler: Strong pull from behavior scoring system that already exists but lacks reasoning data.
    88→- Design implication: Reasoning must be queryable by the Observer agent and behavior scorer programmatically. Not just a UI feature.
    89→
    90→---
    91→
    92→## Job 3: Audit and Compliance
    93→
    94→### Job Story
    95→
    96→**When** reviewing a sequence of agent-authorized actions for compliance (e.g., an intent was authorized that spent budget, or an observation led to an automated learning proposal),
    97→**I want to** see the full provenance chain -- from intent reasoning through evaluation through observation reasoning through learning proposal,
    98→**so I can** verify that every step in the autonomous chain was justified by sound logic and evidence.
    99→
   100→### Functional Job
   101→Trace the complete reasoning chain from intent submission through authorization evaluation through observation creation through learning diagnosis, with LLM reasoning visible at each node.
   102→
   103→### Emotional Job
   104→Feel assured that the autonomous system is auditable and that no agent action happened without traceable justification.
   105→
   106→### Social Job
   107→Demonstrate to external auditors, compliance reviewers, or skeptical stakeholders that autonomous agent actions have complete, machine-readable provenance.
   108→
   109→### Four Forces Analysis
   110→
   111→| Force | Description |
   112→|-------|-------------|
   113→| **Push** | The intent table stores human `reasoning` and the trace table stores model stats, but the LLM's actual chain-of-thought for authorization evaluation (`evaluation.reason` is a one-line summary) is not captured. For observations, the `text` field is the agent's output, not its reasoning process. Auditors cannot distinguish "what the agent concluded" from "how it got there." |
   114→| **Pull** | Full provenance: Intent has human reasoning + LLM authorization reasoning. Observation has agent conclusion text + LLM verification reasoning. Trace has model stats. Together: complete audit trail from intent to action to verification to learning. |
   115→| **Anxiety** | "Will reasoning storage create legal liability?" / "Will it be used against us if reasoning contains errors?" / "Is this GDPR-relevant if reasoning mentions user names?" |
   116→| **Habit** | Currently, `evaluation.reason` on intent is the only LLM reasoning persisted, and it's a one-line summary. Teams treat this as sufficient. Changing the expectation to full chain-of-thought requires culture shift. |
   117→
   118→### Assessment
   119→- Switch likelihood: **Medium** -- habit of treating one-line summaries as sufficient is real. Push becomes stronger as the system handles higher-stakes actions.
   120→- Key blocker: Habit of treating summary-level reasoning as "good enough."
   121→- Key enabler: Pull from increasing autonomy scope (budget-spending intents, automated learning proposals) making full audit trails non-negotiable.
   122→- Design implication: LLM reasoning on intents needs a distinct field from human `reasoning`. Naming must be unambiguous: `llm_reasoning` vs existing `reasoning`.
   123→
   124→---
   125→
   126→## Opportunity Scoring
   127→
   128→| # | Outcome Statement | Imp. (%) | Sat. (%) | Score | Priority |
   129→|---|-------------------|----------|----------|-------|----------|
   130→| 1 | Minimize the time to determine why an agent created a specific observation | 95 | 15 | 17.6 | Extremely Underserved |
   131→| 2 | Minimize the likelihood of undetected reasoning quality degradation | 85 | 10 | 16.0 | Extremely Underserved |
   132→| 3 | Maximize the likelihood that every autonomous action has traceable justification | 90 | 40 | 14.0 | Underserved |
   133→| 4 | Minimize the time to trace provenance from intent through observation to learning | 80 | 25 | 12.4 | Underserved |
   134→| 5 | Minimize the likelihood of exposing internal reasoning to unauthorized consumers | 75 | 60 | 9.0 | Overserved (existing access control patterns) |
   135→| 6 | Minimize the storage cost of persisting LLM reasoning | 60 | 70 | 6.0 | Overserved |
   136→
   137→### Scoring Method
   138→- Importance: estimated % of workspace admins/Observer use cases rating 4+ on 5-point scale
   139→- Satisfaction: estimated % satisfied with current state (log-grepping, one-line summaries)
   140→- Score: Importance + max(0, Importance - Satisfaction)
   141→- Source: team estimate based on codebase analysis and issue #154 discussion
   142→- Confidence: Medium (team estimates, not user interviews)
   143→
   144→### Top Opportunities (Score >= 12)
   145→1. **Root cause diagnosis speed** (17.6) -- Store reasoning on observation, expose via "View Logic"
   146→2. **Reasoning drift detection** (16.0) -- Make reasoning queryable for behavior scoring
   147→3. **Full provenance chain** (14.0) -- Add `llm_reasoning` field to intent for authorization chain-of-thought
   148→4. **Cross-entity provenance tracing** (12.4) -- Enable trace navigation from intent -> observation -> learning with reasoning at each node
   149→
   150→### Overserved Areas (Score < 10)
   151→1. **Access control** (9.0) -- Existing workspace-scoped access patterns are sufficient; no new auth model needed
   152→2. **Storage cost** (6.0) -- Reasoning strings are small relative to embeddings already stored; not a real concern
   153→
   154→---
   155→
   156→## Job-to-Story Mapping (Preview)
   157→
   158→| Job | Primary Stories |
   159→|-----|----------------|
   160→| J1: Forensic Debugging | US-01: Persist LLM reasoning on observations, US-03: "View Logic" toggle in observation detail UI |
   161→| J2: Drift Detection | US-01 (shared), US-04: Observer loads reasoning for self-calibration |
   162→| J3: Audit/Compliance | US-02: Persist LLM reasoning on intents, US-05: Provenance chain navigation |
   163→
   164→Note: US-01 serves both J1 and J2 (N:1 mapping). All stories detailed in `docs/requirements/intent-llm-traces/`.
   165→

1→# Shared Artifacts Registry: LLM Reasoning Telemetry
     2→
     3→## Artifacts
     4→
     5→### observation.reasoning (NEW FIELD)
     6→
     7→| Property | Value |
     8→|----------|-------|
     9→| Source of truth | `observation` table, `reasoning` field (TYPE `option<string>`) |
    10→| Owner | Observer verification pipeline (`observer/llm-reasoning.ts`) |
    11→| Consumers | Observation detail UI ("View Logic" panel), Observer self-calibration queries, Behavior scorer reasoning analysis |
    12→| Integration risk | **HIGH** -- this is the core new field. Must be populated by all LLM reasoning paths (verification, peer review, anomaly evaluation, contradiction detection) and consumed correctly by UI and programmatic consumers. |
    13→| Validation | Field populated when `source = "llm"`. Field absent/NONE when `source = "deterministic_fallback"` or observation predates feature. UI must handle both states. |
    14→
    15→### intent.llm_reasoning (NEW FIELD)
    16→
    17→| Property | Value |
    18→|----------|-------|
    19→| Source of truth | `intent` table, `llm_reasoning` field (TYPE `option<string>`) |
    20→| Owner | Intent authorization evaluation pipeline (`oauth/intent-submission.ts`) |
    21→| Consumers | Intent detail UI, Audit provenance chain, Compliance review |
    22→| Integration risk | **HIGH** -- must be clearly distinct from existing `intent.reasoning` (human rationale). Naming must prevent confusion. |
    23→| Validation | Field populated when authorization used LLM evaluation. Field absent/NONE for policy-only evaluations (`evaluation.policy_only = true`). |
    24→
    25→### intent.reasoning (EXISTING FIELD -- unchanged)
    26→
    27→| Property | Value |
    28→|----------|-------|
    29→| Source of truth | `intent` table, `reasoning` field (TYPE `string`, required) |
    30→| Owner | Intent requester (human or agent submitting intent) |
    31→| Consumers | Intent detail UI, audit trail |
    32→| Integration risk | **MEDIUM** -- existing consumers must not confuse this with new `llm_reasoning`. |
    33→| Validation | Already required field. No change needed. |
    34→
    35→### observation.source
    36→
    37→| Property | Value |
    38→|----------|-------|
    39→| Source of truth | `observation` table, `source` field (TYPE `option<string>`) |
    40→| Owner | Verification pipeline (`applyLlmVerdict()`) |
    41→| Consumers | UI "View Logic" panel (determines panel content), Observer queries |
    42→| Integration risk | **MEDIUM** -- UI uses this to decide whether to show LLM reasoning, deterministic fallback explanation, or "no reasoning" message. |
    43→| Validation | Values: `"llm"`, `"deterministic_fallback"`, `"github"`, `"peer_review"`, `"none"`. When `"llm"`: reasoning field should be populated. When `"deterministic_fallback"`: reasoning field should be absent. |
    44→
    45→### trace (EXISTING TABLE -- unchanged, used as link target)
    46→
    47→| Property | Value |
    48→|----------|-------|
    49→| Source of truth | `trace` table |
    50→| Owner | LLM call infrastructure |
    51→| Consumers | Reasoning panel (model stats link), trace hierarchy navigation |
    52→| Integration risk | **LOW** -- existing table, no schema changes. Used as link target from reasoning panel. |
    53→| Validation | Trace must exist for LLM calls. Observation links to trace indirectly via `source_session -> invoked -> trace`. |
    54→
    55→## Integration Validation Checklist
    56→
    57→- [ ] `observation.reasoning` populated by `generateVerificationVerdict()` path in graph-scan.ts
    58→- [ ] `observation.reasoning` populated by `generatePeerReviewVerdict()` path
    59→- [ ] `observation.reasoning` populated by anomaly evaluation path in graph-scan.ts
    60→- [ ] `observation.reasoning` populated by contradiction detection path
    61→- [ ] `observation.reasoning` NOT populated when source is `deterministic_fallback`
    62→- [ ] `intent.llm_reasoning` populated by authorization evaluation when LLM is used
    63→- [ ] `intent.llm_reasoning` NOT populated when `evaluation.policy_only = true`
    64→- [ ] UI "View Logic" panel reads `observation.reasoning` and handles NONE
    65→- [ ] UI "View Logic" panel reads `observation.source` to determine panel content
    66→- [ ] Observer self-calibration queries filter `WHERE reasoning != NONE`
    67→- [ ] Non-admin API responses exclude `reasoning` field from observation payloads
    68→- [ ] `intent.reasoning` (human) remains unchanged and distinct from `intent.llm_reasoning`
    69→

1→journey:
     2→  name: "Investigating a Failed Agent Action"
     3→  goal: "Determine whether an agent's autonomous action was justified by sound reasoning, and take corrective action if not"
     4→  persona: "Carla Navarro, workspace admin managing 3 projects, reviews Observer findings daily"
     5→  jobs_served:
     6→    - "J1: Forensic Debugging"
     7→    - "J2: Drift Detection"
     8→    - "J3: Audit/Compliance"
     9→
    10→  emotional_arc:
    11→    start: "Frustrated/Confused -- agent took unexpected action, no visibility into why"
    12→    middle: "Focused/Hopeful -- reasoning is available, reading chain-of-thought"
    13→    end: "Relieved/Confident -- root cause identified, corrective action clear"
    14→
    15→steps:
    16→  - id: 1
    17→    name: "Trigger: Conflict Observation in Feed"
    18→    action: "Carla reviews governance feed and sees conflict-severity observation"
    19→    actor: "workspace_admin"
    20→
    21→    shared_artifacts:
    22→      - name: "observation_id"
    23→        source: "observation table (SurrealDB)"
    24→        displayed_as: "${observation_id}"

1→# Journey: Investigating a Failed Agent Action
     2→
     3→## Emotional Arc Pattern: Problem Relief
     4→Start: Frustrated/Confused | Middle: Focused/Hopeful | End: Relieved/Confident
     5→
     6→## Actors
     7→
     8→1. **Carla Navarro** -- workspace admin at a 12-person product team using Brain. She manages 3 projects and reviews Observer findings daily in the feed.
     9→2. **Observer Agent** -- autonomous agent that scans the graph for contradictions, staleness, and drift. Consumes reasoning programmatically for self-calibration.
    10→3. **Behavior Scorer** -- evaluates agent behavioral quality over time using scoring functions and trend analysis.
    11→
    12→---
    13→
    14→## Journey Flow (Happy Path)
    15→
    16→```
    17→  [TRIGGER]                [LOCATE]               [EXAMINE]
    18→  Carla sees a         She opens the          She toggles
    19→  conflict observation  observation detail      "View Logic"
    20→  in the feed           to understand scope
    21→       |                     |                      |
    22→       v                     v                      v
    23→  +----------+         +-----------+          +------------+
    24→  | Feed Card|  --->   | Obs Detail|  --->    | LLM        |
    25→  | severity:|         | text,     |          | Reasoning  |
    26→  | conflict |         | evidence, |          | Panel      |
    27→  |          |         | linked    |          |            |
    28→  +----------+         | entities  |          +------------+
    29→                       +-----------+                |
    30→                                                    v
    31→                                              [DIAGNOSE]
    32→                                           She reads the
    33→                                           chain-of-thought
    34→                                           and checks evidence
    35→                                                    |
    36→                                                    v
    37→                                              +------------+
    38→                                              | Decision:  |
    39→                                              | Valid find |
    40→                                              | or false   |
    41→                                              | positive?  |
    42→                                              +-----+------+
    43→                                                    |
    44→                                        +-----------+-----------+
    45→                                        |                       |
    46→                                        v                       v
    47→                                  [RESOLVE]               [CORRECT]
    48→                              Acknowledge the          Resolve as false
    49→                              valid finding            positive, note for
    50→                              and create task          drift tracking
    51→                                        |                       |
    52→                                        v                       v
    53→                                  +-----------+          +-----------+
    54→                                  | Task      |          | Resolved  |
    55→                                  | Created   |          | + Pattern |
    56→                                  |           |          | Noted     |
    57→                                  +-----------+          +-----------+
    58→```
    59→
    60→---
    61→
    62→## Step-by-Step with Emotional Annotations
    63→
    64→### Step 1: Trigger -- Conflict Observation Surfaces in Feed
    65→
    66→**Actor**: Carla Navarro
    67→**Action**: Reviews governance feed, sees a conflict-severity observation
    68→
    69→```
    70→+-- Feed -------------------------------------------------------+
    71→|                                                                |
    72→|  [!] CONFLICT  3 min ago                                       |
    73→|  Task "Migrate billing to tRPC" contradicts confirmed          |
    74→|  decision "Standardize on REST for public APIs"                |
    75→|  Observer  |  confidence: 0.82  |  2 evidence refs             |
    76→|                                                                |
    77→|  [!] WARNING  18 min ago                                       |
    78→|  Task "Add Redis caching" blocked for 14 days...               |
    79→|                                                                |
    80→+----------------------------------------------------------------+
    81→```
    82→
    83→**Emotional state**: Frustrated/Concerned -- "The Observer flagged something. Is this real or a false positive? I have no idea WHY it thinks there's a contradiction."
    84→**Exit emotion**: Curious -- "Let me look deeper."
    85→
    86→**Shared artifacts produced**: `${observation_id}`, `${observation_text}`, `${severity}`, `${confidence}`
    87→
    88→---
    89→
    90→### Step 2: Locate -- Open Observation Detail
    91→
    92→**Actor**: Carla Navarro
    93→**Action**: Clicks into observation detail view
    94→
    95→```
    96→+-- Observation Detail ------------------------------------------+
    97→|                                                                |
    98→|  CONFLICT  observation:a1b2c3d4                                |
    99→|  Created: 2026-03-16 09:12 UTC  |  Observer  |  conf: 0.82    |
   100→|                                                                |
   101→|  Task "Migrate billing to tRPC" contradicts confirmed          |
   102→|  decision "Standardize on REST for public APIs"                |
   103→|                                                                |
   104→|  Evidence:                                                     |
   105→|    - decision:e5f6g7h8 "Standardize on REST..." (confirmed)    |
   106→|    - task:i9j0k1l2 "Migrate billing to tRPC" (in_progress)    |
   107→|                                                                |
   108→|  Linked Entities: 2  |  Verified: No  |  Type: contradiction   |
   109→|                                                                |
   110→|  [ View Logic ]  [ Acknowledge ]  [ Resolve ]                  |
   111→|                                                                |
   112→+----------------------------------------------------------------+
   113→```
   114→
   115→**Emotional state**: Focused -- "I can see WHAT was flagged and the evidence refs. But I still do not know the reasoning."
   116→**Exit emotion**: Anticipating -- "The 'View Logic' button is right there."
   117→
   118→**Shared artifacts consumed**: `${observation_id}`, `${observation_text}`, `${evidence_refs}`
   119→**Shared artifacts produced**: `${linked_decision_id}`, `${linked_task_id}`
   120→
   121→---
   122→
   123→### Step 3: Examine -- Toggle "View Logic" to See LLM Reasoning
   124→
   125→**Actor**: Carla Navarro
   126→**Action**: Clicks "View Logic" toggle
   127→
   128→```
   129→+-- Observation Detail ------------------------------------------+
   130→|                                                                |
   131→|  CONFLICT  observation:a1b2c3d4                                |
   132→|  ...                                                           |
   133→|                                                                |
   134→|  +-- LLM Reasoning (internal telemetry) --------------------+  |
   135→|  |                                                          |  |
   136→|  |  The task "Migrate billing to tRPC" (task:i9j0k1l2)     |  |
   137→|  |  explicitly targets the billing API for migration from   |  |
   138→|  |  REST to tRPC. However, decision "Standardize on REST    |  |
   139→|  |  for public APIs" (decision:e5f6g7h8, status: confirmed) |  |
   140→|  |  requires all public-facing APIs to use REST.            |  |
   141→|  |                                                          |  |
   142→|  |  The billing API is public-facing (it serves external    |  |
   143→|  |  payment provider webhooks). Therefore, migrating it     |  |
   144→|  |  to tRPC would violate the confirmed decision.           |  |
   145→|  |                                                          |  |
   146→|  |  Claim: billing API should use tRPC                      |  |
   147→|  |  Reality: confirmed decision requires REST for public    |  |
   148→|  |  APIs                                                    |  |
   149→|  |                                                          |  |
   150→|  |  Confidence: 0.82                                        |  |
   151→|  |  Model: anthropic/claude-sonnet  |  Trace: trace:x1y2z3  |  |
   152→|  +----------------------------------------------------------+  |
   153→|                                                                |
   154→|  [ Hide Logic ]  [ Acknowledge ]  [ Resolve ]                  |
   155→|                                                                |
   156→+----------------------------------------------------------------+
   157→```
   158→
   159→**Emotional state**: Relieved/Illuminated -- "Now I can see EXACTLY why the Observer reached this conclusion. The reasoning is specific -- it identified the billing API as public-facing and connected that to the REST decision."
   160→**Exit emotion**: Confident -- "This is a valid finding. The reasoning checks out."
   161→
   162→**Shared artifacts consumed**: `${observation_id}`, `${reasoning}`, `${trace_id}`
   163→**New data shown**: `${llm_reasoning_text}`, `${model_identifier}`, `${trace_link}`
   164→
   165→---
   166→
   167→### Step 4: Diagnose -- Evaluate Reasoning Validity
   168→
   169→**Actor**: Carla Navarro
   170→**Action**: Reads reasoning, cross-references with linked decision and task
   171→
   172→**Decision tree**:
   173→- If reasoning is sound and evidence supports the claim: proceed to Step 5a (Acknowledge)
   174→- If reasoning has a flaw (e.g., billing API is actually internal-only): proceed to Step 5b (Resolve as false positive)
   175→
   176→**Emotional state**: Analytical -- applying judgment to the LLM's reasoning.
   177→**Exit emotion**: Decided -- "I know what to do."
   178→
   179→---
   180→
   181→### Step 5a: Resolve (Valid Finding) -- Acknowledge and Act
   182→
   183→**Actor**: Carla Navarro
   184→**Action**: Clicks "Acknowledge", then creates a task to address the contradiction
   185→
   186→**Emotional state**: Productive -- "The system caught a real issue. I can act on it."
   187→**Exit emotion**: Satisfied -- "The autonomous system works. It caught something humans missed."
   188→
   189→---
   190→
   191→### Step 5b: Correct (False Positive) -- Resolve and Note Pattern
   192→
   193→**Actor**: Carla Navarro
   194→**Action**: Clicks "Resolve" with resolution note
   195→
   196→**Emotional state**: Mildly frustrated -- "False positive, but at least I could diagnose it in 2 minutes instead of 30."
   197→**Exit emotion**: Constructive -- "This false positive pattern should be tracked for drift detection."
   198→
   199→---
   200→
   201→## Parallel Journey: Observer Self-Calibration (Programmatic)
   202→
   203→```
   204→  [LOAD]                    [COMPARE]                [SIGNAL]
   205→  Observer loads             Compares reasoning       Creates observation
   206→  recent observation         quality across time      about own drift
   207→  reasoning in context       window
   208→       |                         |                        |
   209→       v                         v                        v
   210→  query observation       pattern detection:        observation:
   211→  WHERE workspace=$ws     - reasoning length         "Verification
   212→  AND reasoning != NONE   - evidence density          reasoning quality
   213→  ORDER BY created_at     - confidence clustering     declining: avg
   214→  LIMIT 50                                            confidence 0.72
   215→                                                      vs 0.85 last
   216→                                                      month"
   217→```
   218→
   219→**Actor**: Observer Agent (programmatic)
   220→**Shared artifacts consumed**: `${reasoning}` field on observation records
   221→**Output**: New observation flagging reasoning quality drift, or behavior score input
   222→
   223→---
   224→
   225→## Error Paths
   226→
   227→### E1: Reasoning Field Is Empty (Legacy Observation)
   228→
   229→Observations created before this feature have no `reasoning` field. The "View Logic" toggle should handle this gracefully.
   230→
   231→```
   232→+-- LLM Reasoning (internal telemetry) -----------------------+
   233→|                                                              |
   234→|  No reasoning recorded for this observation.                 |
   235→|  Observations created before [date] do not include           |
   236→|  LLM chain-of-thought.                                      |
   237→|                                                              |
   238→|  Trace data may still be available: trace:x1y2z3             |
   239→|                                                              |
   240→+--------------------------------------------------------------+
   241→```
   242→
   243→### E2: LLM Call Failed During Verification
   244→
   245→When `generateVerificationVerdict()` returns `undefined`, the observation is created with `source: "deterministic_fallback"` and no LLM reasoning. The UI should indicate the reasoning source.
   246→
   247→```
   248→+-- LLM Reasoning (internal telemetry) -----------------------+
   249→|                                                              |
   250→|  Reasoning unavailable: verification used deterministic      |
   251→|  fallback (LLM call failed or timed out).                    |
   252→|                                                              |
   253→|  Deterministic verdict: mismatch                             |
   254→|  Source: CI status check                                     |
   255→|                                                              |
   256→+--------------------------------------------------------------+
   257→```
   258→
   259→### E3: Reasoning Exists But Trace Is Missing
   260→
   261→The trace link on the reasoning panel may point to a trace that was pruned or failed to persist.
   262→
   263→```
   264→  Trace: trace:x1y2z3 (not found)
   265→```
   266→
   267→---
   268→
   269→## Integration Points
   270→
   271→| From Step | To Step | Data Passed | Validation |
   272→|-----------|---------|-------------|------------|
   273→| Observer verification pipeline | Observation creation | `reasoning` string from `LlmVerdict.reasoning` | Must not be empty string when LLM was called successfully |
   274→| Observation record | UI "View Logic" panel | `reasoning` field from observation table | Handle missing field for legacy observations |
   275→| Observation record | Observer self-calibration | `reasoning` field via query | Query must filter `reasoning != NONE` for analysis |
   276→| Intent authorization | Intent record | `llm_reasoning` from evaluation pipeline | Distinct from existing `reasoning` (human rationale) |
   277→| Observation `trace_id` | Trace detail | Model stats link | Optional -- observation may not have direct trace_id |
   278→

1→Feature: Investigating a Failed Agent Action
     2→  As a workspace admin investigating autonomous agent actions,
     3→  I want to see the LLM's chain-of-thought reasoning behind observations and intent evaluations,
     4→  so I can diagnose whether the agent reasoned correctly and take corrective action.
     5→
     6→  Background:
     7→    Given workspace "Acme Product" managed by Carla Navarro
     8→    And the Observer agent has completed a graph scan
     9→    And the Observer used LLM verification for entity "task:i9j0k1l2" against "decision:e5f6g7h8"
    10→
    11→  # --- Job 1: Forensic Debugging (Happy Path) ---
    12→
    13→  Scenario: LLM reasoning persisted on observation during verification
    14→    Given the Observer's generateVerificationVerdict returned a verdict with reasoning
    15→      """
    16→      The task "Migrate billing to tRPC" (task:i9j0k1l2) explicitly targets the billing API
    17→      for migration from REST to tRPC. However, decision "Standardize on REST for public APIs"
    18→      (decision:e5f6g7h8, status: confirmed) requires all public-facing APIs to use REST.
    19→      The billing API is public-facing (it serves external payment provider webhooks).
    20→      Therefore, migrating it to tRPC would violate the confirmed decision.
    21→      """
    22→    When the Observer creates an observation from the verification result
    23→    Then the observation record includes a "reasoning" field containing the LLM chain-of-thought
    24→    And the observation "text" field contains the agent's conclusion (distinct from reasoning)
    25→    And the observation "confidence" is 0.82
    26→    And the observation "source" is "llm"
    27→    And the observation "evidence_refs" includes "decision:e5f6g7h8" and "task:i9j0k1l2"
    28→
    29→  Scenario: Workspace admin views LLM reasoning via "View Logic" toggle
    30→    Given observation "observation:a1b2c3d4" has a "reasoning" field populated
    31→    And Carla Navarro is viewing the observation detail page
    32→    When Carla clicks "View Logic"
    33→    Then the reasoning panel expands showing the full LLM chain-of-thought
    34→    And the panel displays the model identifier from the linked trace
    35→    And the panel shows a link to the trace record for model stats
    36→    And the reasoning text references entity IDs that match evidence_refs
    37→
    38→  Scenario: Admin diagnoses valid finding and acknowledges
    39→    Given Carla has read the LLM reasoning for observation "observation:a1b2c3d4"
    40→    And the reasoning correctly identifies the billing API as public-facing
    41→    When Carla clicks "Acknowledge"
    42→    Then the observation status changes to "acknowledged"
    43→    And Carla can create a remediation task linked to the observation
    44→
    45→  # --- Job 1: Forensic Debugging (Error Paths) ---
    46→
    47→  Scenario: Legacy observation without reasoning shows graceful fallback
    48→    Given observation "observation:legacy001" was created before the reasoning feature
    49→    And the observation has no "reasoning" field
    50→    When Carla opens the observation detail
    51→    Then the "View Logic" toggle is visible but dimmed
    52→    And clicking it shows "No reasoning recorded for this observation"
    53→    And the trace link is shown as a fallback for model stats
    54→
    55→  Scenario: Deterministic fallback observation shows source explanation
    56→    Given observation "observation:det001" has source "deterministic_fallback"
    57→    And the observation has no LLM reasoning (LLM call failed)
    58→    When Carla clicks "View Logic"
    59→    Then the panel shows "Reasoning unavailable: verification used deterministic fallback"
    60→    And the deterministic verdict details are displayed
    61→    And the CI status source is shown
    62→
    63→  Scenario: Reasoning exists but linked trace is missing
    64→    Given observation "observation:a1b2c3d4" has reasoning populated
    65→    And the linked trace "trace:x1y2z3" has been pruned
    66→    When Carla views the reasoning panel
    67→    Then the reasoning text is fully displayed
    68→    And the trace link shows "(not found)"
    69→    And model stats are shown as "unavailable"
    70→
    71→  # --- Job 2: Drift Detection (Programmatic) ---
    72→
    73→  Scenario: Observer queries reasoning for self-calibration
    74→    Given 50 observations exist in workspace "Acme Product" with reasoning populated
    75→    And the observations span the last 30 days
    76→    When the Observer agent queries observations with reasoning for drift analysis
    77→    Then the query returns observations with reasoning, confidence, and created_at fields
    78→    And observations without reasoning (legacy or fallback) are excluded
    79→    And the Observer can compute reasoning quality metrics across the time window
    80→
    81→  Scenario: Behavior scorer evaluates Observer reasoning quality
    82→    Given behavior definition "Observer Verification Quality" exists for workspace "Acme Product"
    83→    And 20 recent observations have reasoning populated
    84→    When the behavior scorer evaluates the Observer agent
    85→    Then the scorer loads observation reasoning as scoring input
    86→    And the score reflects reasoning specificity (evidence references, entity mentions)
    87→    And the trend is compared against the previous scoring period
    88→
    89→  # --- Job 3: Audit/Compliance ---
    90→
    91→  Scenario: LLM reasoning persisted on intent authorization evaluation
    92→    Given intent "intent:auth001" for action "deploy billing service"
    93→    And the Authorizer agent evaluated the intent with LLM reasoning
    94→      """
    95→      Intent requests deployment of billing service. Policy "deploy-approval"
    96→      requires human approval for production deployments. The intent's priority (75)
    97→      exceeds the auto-approve threshold (50) in the policy. Risk score: 65.
    98→      Recommending APPROVE with human veto window.
    99→      """
   100→    When the intent evaluation is persisted
   101→    Then the intent record includes an "llm_reasoning" field with the authorization chain-of-thought
   102→    And the existing "reasoning" field retains the human-provided rationale
   103→    And the "evaluation.reason" field retains the one-line summary
   104→
   105→  Scenario: Full provenance chain from intent through observation to learning
   106→    Given intent "intent:auth001" has "llm_reasoning" populated
   107→    And the intent's execution produced observation "observation:a1b2c3d4" with "reasoning" populated
   108→    And the observation triggered learning diagnosis with "reasoning" in rootCauseSchema
   109→    When Carla navigates the provenance chain
   110→    Then each node (intent, observation, learning proposal) shows its LLM reasoning
   111→    And the trace hierarchy links all three via parent_trace relationships
   112→    And model stats are available at each trace node
   113→
   114→  # --- Properties ---
   115→
   116→  @property
   117→  Scenario: Reasoning field does not duplicate observation text
   118→    Given any observation created by the Observer with LLM verification
   119→    Then the "reasoning" field contains the LLM chain-of-thought (how the agent got there)
   120→    And the "text" field contains the observation conclusion (what the agent concluded)
   121→    And the two fields are semantically distinct
   122→
   123→  @property
   124→  Scenario: Reasoning is internal telemetry not exposed to non-admin consumers
   125→    Given observation "observation:a1b2c3d4" with reasoning populated
   126→    When a non-Observer agent loads the observation via search_entities or get_entity_detail
   127→    Then the response includes "text", "severity", "confidence" fields
   128→    And the "reasoning" field is not included in the response
   129→    And only workspace admins and the Observer agent can access reasoning
   130→
   131→  @property
   132→  Scenario: Reasoning storage does not duplicate model stats from trace table
   133→    Given an observation created from an LLM verification call
   134→    Then the observation stores only the reasoning text (string)
   135→    And model, tokens, cost, and latency are stored exclusively on the linked trace record
   136→    And no model_stats object exists on the observation or intent tables
   137→
