toolUseResult.content:
# LLM Reasoning as Internal Telemetry — Implementation Roadmap
#
# Store LLM chain-of-thought reasoning on Observation and Intent nodes.
# Extends Observer verification pipeline and Intent authorization to persist
# reasoning strings, adds query function for programmatic consumers, and
# gates reasoning fields to admin-only API responses.
#
# Design artifacts:
#   - docs/feature/intent-llm-traces/design/architecture-design.md
#   - docs/adrs/ADR-053-llm-reasoning-as-internal-telemetry.md
#   - docs/requirements/intent-llm-traces/US-01 through US-04
#   - tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/ (37 acceptance scenarios)
#
# Paradigm: Functional (TypeScript)
# Rigor: inherit model, review enabled, mutation disabled

project_id: intent-llm-traces
title: "LLM Reasoning as Internal Telemetry on Intent and Observation Nodes"
paradigm: functional

roadmap:
  project_id: intent-llm-traces
  created_at: "2026-03-16"
  total_steps: 4

validation:
  status: pending

phases:
  - id: "01"
    name: "Observation Reasoning Persistence"
    title: "Schema + Observer Pipeline Threading"
    description: >
      Add reasoning field to observation table, extend createObservation to accept
      reasoning parameter, and thread reasoning from all Observer LLM paths through
      to persistence. This is the critical path unblocking steps 03 and 04.
    steps:
      - id: "01-01"
        step_id: "01-01"
        name: "Schema migration and observation reasoning persistence"
        title: "Schema migration and observation reasoning persistence"
        description: >
          Add `reasoning` field (option<string>) to observation table via schema migration.
          Extend `createObservation()` in observation/queries.ts to accept optional reasoning
          parameter and persist it. Thread reasoning from all Observer LLM paths:
          (1) generateVerificationVerdict -> applyLlmVerdict -> persistObservation -> createObservation,
          (2) generatePeerReviewVerdict -> persistObservation -> createObservation,
          (3) detectContradictions -> runGraphScan contradiction loop -> createObservation,
          (4) evaluateAnomalies -> runGraphScan anomaly loop -> createObservation,
          (5) verifyContradiction -> trace-response-analyzer -> createObservation,
          (6) verifyMissingDecision -> trace-response-analyzer -> createObservation.
          Deterministic observations (source=deterministic_fallback) must NOT have reasoning.
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "observation table has DEFINE FIELD reasoning ON observation TYPE option<string> via schema migration using DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE"
          - "surreal-schema.surql updated with reasoning field definition on observation table"
          - "createObservation() in observation/queries.ts accepts optional reasoning?: string parameter and includes it in the CREATE statement when provided"
          - "Observer verification path: LlmVerdict.reasoning from generateVerificationVerdict flows through applyLlmVerdict -> VerificationResult -> persistObservation -> createObservation"
          - "Observer peer review path: PeerReviewVerdict.reasoning flows through persistObservation -> createObservation"
          - "Graph scan contradiction path: DetectedContradiction.reasoning flows through runGraphScan contradiction loop -> createObservation"
          - "Graph scan anomaly path: AnomalyEvaluation.reasoning flows through runGraphScan anomaly loop -> createObservation"
          - "Trace analyzer paths: verifyContradiction and verifyMissingDecision reasoning flows through their respective createObservation calls"
          - "Deterministic observations (shouldSkipLlm=true) do NOT have reasoning populated"
          - "observation.text (conclusion) and observation.reasoning (chain-of-thought) remain semantically distinct — reasoning is NOT copied into text"
          - "bun migrate applies the migration successfully"
          - "Existing observations without reasoning continue to load (backward compatible)"
        test_file: "tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/observation-reasoning.test.ts"
        files_to_modify:
          - "schema/migrations/NEXT_observation_reasoning.surql"
          - "schema/surreal-schema.surql"
          - "app/src/server/observation/queries.ts"
          - "app/src/server/agents/observer/agent.ts"
          - "app/src/server/observer/verification-pipeline.ts"
          - "app/src/server/observer/graph-scan.ts"
          - "app/src/server/observer/llm-reasoning.ts"
          - "app/src/server/observer/llm-synthesis.ts"
          - "app/src/server/observer/trace-response-analyzer.ts"
        implementation_notes: >
          Check the next migration number by scanning schema/migrations/ for the highest prefix.
          Use DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE for the migration. Wrap in BEGIN TRANSACTION / COMMIT TRANSACTION.
          The VerificationResult type (verification-pipeline.ts) needs a reasoning?: string field added.
          persistObservation (agent.ts) must extract reasoning from VerificationResult and pass to createObservation.
          In graph-scan.ts, the contradiction and anomaly loops already iterate over LLM results — thread .reasoning to createObservation calls.
          In trace-response-analyzer.ts, verify that verifyContradiction and verifyMissingDecision return objects with reasoning fields.
        dependencies: []

  - id: "02"
    name: "Intent Reasoning Persistence"
    title: "Authorizer LLM Chain-of-Thought Capture"
    description: >
      Add llm_reasoning field to intent table, extend LLM evaluator to capture
      full chain-of-thought alongside existing reason summary, and thread through
      evaluateIntent to updateIntentStatus.
    steps:
      - id: "02-01"
        step_id: "02-01"
        name: "Intent LLM reasoning persistence"
        title: "Intent LLM reasoning persistence"
        description: >
          Add `llm_reasoning` field (option<string>) to intent table via schema migration.
          Extend the LLM evaluator Zod schema (evaluationResultSchema in authorizer.ts) to
          include a `reasoning` field for full chain-of-thought alongside existing `reason`
          (one-line summary). Thread the reasoning through evaluateIntent return value to
          updateIntentStatus as `llm_reasoning`. Policy-only evaluations (no LLM call) must
          NOT have llm_reasoning populated.
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "intent table has DEFINE FIELD llm_reasoning ON intent TYPE option<string> via schema migration using DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE"
          - "surreal-schema.surql updated with llm_reasoning field definition on intent table"
          - "evaluationResultSchema in authorizer.ts extended with reasoning field (full chain-of-thought) distinct from existing reason (summary)"
          - "evaluateIntent returns llm_reasoning from LLM evaluator result"
          - "updateIntentStatus in intent-queries.ts accepts and persists llm_reasoning on the intent record"
          - "Policy-only evaluations (evaluation.policy_only=true) do NOT have llm_reasoning populated"
          - "Existing intent.reasoning field (human rationale) is unchanged"
          - "Existing intents without llm_reasoning continue to load (backward compatible)"
          - "bun migrate applies the migration successfully"
        test_file: "tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/intent-reasoning.test.ts"
        files_to_modify:
          - "schema/migrations/NEXT_intent_llm_reasoning.surql"
          - "schema/surreal-schema.surql"
          - "app/src/server/intent/authorizer.ts"
          - "app/src/server/intent/intent-queries.ts"
          - "app/src/server/intent/types.ts"
          - "app/src/server/oauth/intent-submission.ts"
        implementation_notes: >
          The evaluationResultSchema Zod schema needs a new `reasoning` field with .describe()
          instructing the LLM to provide full chain-of-thought reasoning. The existing `reason`
          field should keep its current description (brief summary). StatusUpdateFields type
          in intent-queries.ts needs llm_reasoning?: string added. The intent-submission.ts
          handler must pass evaluation.reasoning through to updateIntentStatus as llm_reasoning.
        dependencies: []

  - id: "03"
    name: "Reasoning Query and API Gating"
    title: "Programmatic Access and Access Control"
    description: >
      Add listObservationsWithReasoning query function for Observer self-calibration
      and behavior scorer. Gate reasoning fields in observation/intent detail API
      responses to admin-role requesters only.
    steps:
      - id: "03-01"
        step_id: "03-01"
        name: "Reasoning query function and API access control"
        title: "Reasoning query function and API access control"
        description: >
          Add listObservationsWithReasoning() query function in observation/queries.ts.
          Returns observations where reasoning IS NOT NONE, filtered by workspace, with
          configurable limit and optional sinceDate. Used by Observer self-calibration
          and behavior scorer (internal server-side only, no new API endpoint).
          Gate reasoning/llm_reasoning fields in observation detail and intent detail
          API responses to admin-role requesters only. Non-admin responses omit the
          fields entirely (not null, per project convention).
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "listObservationsWithReasoning() returns observations with reasoning, confidence, source, evidence_refs, created_at"
          - "Query filters out observations where reasoning is NONE"
          - "Query respects workspace scope via bound parameter"
          - "Query supports configurable limit (default 50) and optional sinceDate parameter"
          - "Query uses existing workspace B-tree index, no KNN"
          - "Results ordered by created_at DESC"
          - "Observation detail API response includes reasoning field for admin-role requesters"
          - "Observation detail API response omits reasoning field for non-admin requesters"
          - "Intent detail API response includes llm_reasoning field for admin-role requesters"
          - "Intent detail API response omits llm_reasoning field for non-admin requesters"
          - "Non-admin responses do not contain null for reasoning fields — fields are omitted entirely"
        test_file: "tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/reasoning-queries.test.ts"
        files_to_modify:
          - "app/src/server/observation/queries.ts"
          - "app/src/server/entities/entity-detail-route.ts"
          - "app/src/server/intent/intent-routes.ts"
        implementation_notes: >
          SurrealQL for the query: SELECT id, text, reasoning, severity, confidence, source,
          observation_type, evidence_refs, created_at FROM observation WHERE workspace = $ws
          AND reasoning IS NOT NONE AND created_at > $since ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT $limit.
          For API gating, check the requester's role at the response serialization layer.
          The observation detail is likely in entity-detail-route.ts — find where entity data
          is returned and conditionally include/exclude the reasoning field based on role.
          For intents, similar gating in intent-routes.ts where intent detail is returned.
        dependencies:
          - "01-01"

  - id: "04"
    name: "UI Reasoning Panel"
    title: "View Logic Collapsible Panel"
    description: >
      Collapsible panel on observation and intent detail views showing LLM reasoning
      with model identifier and trace link. Three states: reasoning available,
      deterministic fallback, legacy empty.
    steps:
      - id: "04-01"
        step_id: "04-01"
        name: "View Logic UI reasoning panel"
        title: "View Logic UI reasoning panel"
        description: >
          Add a collapsible "View Logic" panel to observation detail and intent detail
          views. Panel collapsed by default (reasoning is internal telemetry). Three states:
          (1) Reasoning available — show full text, model identifier from linked trace, trace link.
          (2) Deterministic — show "No LLM reasoning — deterministic observation" message.
          (3) Legacy/empty — show "Reasoning not available" message.
          Model identifier resolved via trace linkage, not stored on observation.
          Panel only renders when API response includes reasoning (admin-only gating from 03-01).
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "View Logic toggle button visible on observation detail for admin users"
          - "Reasoning panel collapsed by default"
          - "Panel shows full reasoning text when reasoning field is present"
          - "Panel shows deterministic fallback message when source is deterministic_fallback and reasoning is absent"
          - "Panel shows legacy empty state message when reasoning is absent and source is not deterministic"
          - "Panel renders in monospace font for reasoning text (code-block style)"
          - "View Logic toggle button visible on intent detail for admin users"
          - "Intent panel shows llm_reasoning text distinct from human reasoning field"
          - "Toggle hides panel on second click"
        test_file: "tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/observation-reasoning.test.ts"
        files_to_modify:
          - "app/src/client/components/observation-detail.tsx"
          - "app/src/client/components/intent-detail.tsx"
          - "app/src/client/components/reasoning-panel.tsx"
        implementation_notes: >
          Create a shared ReasoningPanel component that handles the three states.
          Use shadcn/ui Collapsible component. The panel receives reasoning, source, and
          optional trace info as props. The observation-detail and intent-detail components
          conditionally render ReasoningPanel when reasoning/llm_reasoning is present in
          the API response (which only happens for admins).
          For trace linkage, the API may need to return model identifier from the linked trace
          alongside reasoning — check if entity detail already includes trace info.
        dependencies:
          - "03-01"

- Step ID format: 01-01, 02-01, 03-01, 04-01 — all `^\d{2}-\d{2}$` ✓

Let me dispatch the roadmap review, then start execution.

content.text:
Now let me compile my comprehensive review of the roadmap. I have gathered enough information to provide a detailed verdict.

## ROADMAP REVIEW VERDICT

```yaml
review:
  verdict: REJECTED
  iteration: 1
  
validation_results:
  
  criterion_1_step_ids:
    status: PASS
    details: "All step IDs follow NN-NN format: 01-01, 02-01, 03-01, 04-01"
    
  criterion_2_acceptance_criteria:
    status: BLOCKER
    details: "Acceptance criteria are implementation-prescriptive, not behavioral"
    examples:
      - ac_54: "observation table has DEFINE FIELD reasoning ON observation TYPE option<string> via schema migration using DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE"
        issue: "Prescribes exact SQL syntax instead of observable outcome"
        should_be: "Observation records can carry reasoning text that persists and is retrievable"
      - ac_56: "createObservation() in observation/queries.ts accepts optional reasoning?: string parameter and includes it in the CREATE statement when provided"
        issue: "Prescribes function signature and internal implementation instead of observable behavior"
        should_be: "Observations created via the observation creation interface can include reasoning"
      - ac_57: "Observer verification path: LlmVerdict.reasoning from generateVerificationVerdict flows through applyLlmVerdict -> VerificationResult -> persistObservation -> createObservation"
        issue: "Couples test to internal implementation details (type names, function call chain)"
        should_be: "Observer verification path produces observations with reasoning attached"
      - ac_152: "listObservationsWithReasoning() returns observations with reasoning, confidence, source, evidence_refs, created_at"
        issue: "Prescribes function name and exact fields returned instead of observable outcome"
        should_be: "Query function filters observations to return only those with reasoning, respecting workspace scope"
    severity: BLOCKER
    
  criterion_3_files_to_modify:
    status: BLOCKER
    details: "Step 01-01 lists 9 files with architectural concern; Step 04-01 lists wrong test file"
    findings:
      - file_01_01_completeness: "ISSUE: 9 files for a single step is excessive without decomposition"
        files: "[schema/migrations/NEXT.surql, surreal-schema.surql, observation/queries.ts, agents/observer/agent.ts, verification-pipeline.ts, graph-scan.ts, llm-reasoning.ts, llm-synthesis.ts, trace-response-analyzer.ts]"
        analysis: "While the note justifies this due to 6 LLM paths sharing createObservation entry point, the files_to_modify list represents multiple distinct responsibilities: schema migration, query layer, and 6 different observer modules. This suggests the step may be decomposable."
        recommendation: "Either (1) prove this step is truly atomic and cannot be further decomposed, or (2) split into substeps: 01-01 (schema + query contract), 01-02 (verification path), 01-03 (peer review), 01-04 (graph scan), 01-05 (trace analyzer). Test budget allows for substeps."
        severity: BLOCKER
      - file_04_01_wrong_testfile: "Step 04-01 (UI panel) references observation-reasoning.test.ts, which is the Step 01 test file"
        path_in_roadmap: "test_file: tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/observation-reasoning.test.ts"
        actual_content: "That file tests Step 01 schema/persistence, not Step 04 UI"
        should_be: "tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/view-logic-ui.test.ts or observation-reasoning-ui.test.ts"
        severity: BLOCKER
    severity: BLOCKER
    
  criterion_4_dependencies:
    status: PASS
    details: "Dependency chain is correct and acyclic"
    chain: "01-01 (no deps) -> 02-01 (no deps) -> 03-01 (depends on 01-01) -> 04-01 (depends on 03-01)"
    rationale_sound: "true - 03-01 requires reasoning fields from 01-01 to gate; 04-01 requires API gating from 03-01"
    
  criterion_5_step_scope:
    status: BLOCKER
    details: "Step 01-01 likely not right-sized for single TDD cycle"
    analysis: |
      Step 01-01 requires:
      1. Schema migration file creation
      2. Schema definition update
      3. Query function signature change
      4. Threading reasoning through 6 distinct LLM paths:
         - generateVerificationVerdict path (llm-reasoning.ts)
         - generatePeerReviewVerdict path (llm-reasoning.ts)
         - detectContradictions path (llm-synthesis.ts, graph-scan.ts)
         - evaluateAnomalies path (llm-synthesis.ts, graph-scan.ts)
         - verifyContradiction path (trace-response-analyzer.ts)
         - verifyMissingDecision path (trace-response-analyzer.ts)
      
      In a single TDD cycle, RED phase tests all 6 paths failing. GREEN phase implements
      all 6 paths. This is 6x the typical effort for one step.
      
      Recommendation: Decompose into substeps that each thread one or two paths:
      - 01-01: Schema + query contract + verification path
      - 01-02: Peer review path  
      - 01-03: Graph scan contradiction + anomaly paths
      - 01-04: Trace analyzer paths
      
      Each substep would be testable independently and achievable in one cycle.
    severity: BLOCKER
    
  criterion_6_ac_not_coupling_to_internals:
    status: BLOCKER
    details: "Multiple ACs couple tests to private methods and internal types"
    examples:
      - ac_57: "LlmVerdict.reasoning from generateVerificationVerdict flows through applyLlmVerdict -> VerificationResult"
        issue: "Tests are coupled to internal type names (VerificationResult, LlmVerdict) and function names (applyLlmVerdict)"
        impact: "Refactoring these internal types/functions breaks tests even if behavior is preserved"
      - ac_59: "PeerReviewVerdict.reasoning flows through persistObservation -> createObservation"
        issue: "Tests coupled to internal type PeerReviewVerdict and function persistObservation"
      - ac_60: "DetectedContradiction.reasoning flows through runGraphScan contradiction loop"
        issue: "Tests coupled to internal type DetectedContradiction and loop structure"
      - ac_108: "evaluationResultSchema in authorizer.ts extended with reasoning field"
        issue: "Tests coupled to internal Zod schema name and location"
      - ac_156: "Query uses existing workspace B-tree index, no KNN"
        issue: "Implementation detail; should not constrain how query is implemented"
    severity: BLOCKER
    
  architecture_alignment:
    status: ISSUE
    details: "Architecture design and roadmap have semantic misalignment"
    findings:
      - design_says_6_paths: "Architecture diagram (Level 3) shows 6 distinct LLM reasoning paths"
      - design_allows_flexibility: "Design says 'All 6 paths pass reasoning as separate parameter to createObservation' but doesn't prescribe how"
      - roadmap_overtightens: "Roadmap acceptance criteria prescribe exact call chains and type names, removing implementation flexibility"
      - mismatch: "Design allows implementation flexibility; roadmap removes it by coupling to internals"
    
  test_file_analysis:
    status: PARTIAL
    details: "Test files exist but coverage vs roadmap needs alignment"
    observation_reasoning_test:
      scenarios: 11
      test_types: "[Walking Skeleton, Happy Path (4), Deterministic Path (2), Error Cases (5)]"
      assessment: "Tests are behavioral (create observation, verify fields exist, check data integrity) but some are too implementation-heavy (checking exact function call chains)"
    intent_reasoning_test:
      scenarios: 12
      test_types: "[Walking Skeleton, Happy Path (4), Policy-Only Path (2), Error Cases (6)]"
      assessment: "Behavioral focus; tests check intent record state, not internal implementation"
    reasoning_queries_test:
      scenarios: 14
      test_types: "[Walking Skeleton, Query Function (4), Workspace Scope (1), Edge Cases (5), API Control (2 skipped)]"
      assessment: "Tests query behavior correctly; API access control tests are skipped pending implementation"
    
  external_validity:
    status: PASS
    details: "Acceptance tests drive through public entry points"
    observation_path: "Direct DB access via createObservation() query function and SELECT queries"
    intent_path: "Direct DB access via updateIntentStatus() query function and SELECT queries"
    api_path: "HTTP API endpoints (observation detail, intent detail) — tested via fetchObservationsApi helper"
    
defects:
  
  - id: D1
    severity: BLOCKER
    dimension: "Acceptance Criteria - Implementation Prescriptiveness"
    location: "roadmap.yaml:53-65 (Step 01-01), and similar in 02-01, 03-01, 04-01"
    description: |
      Acceptance criteria prescribe internal implementation details instead of observable outcomes.
      Examples: "DEFINE FIELD ... via schema migration using DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE" (SQL syntax),
      "createObservation() in observation/queries.ts accepts optional reasoning?: string" (function signature),
      "LlmVerdict.reasoning flows through applyLlmVerdict -> VerificationResult" (internal types and call chain).
      
      This couples tests to implementation and violates TDD principle that tests should verify behavior,
      not prescribe how that behavior is achieved.
    suggestion: |
      Rewrite acceptance criteria to focus on observable outcomes:
      - "Observations can be created with optional reasoning text"
      - "Reasoning text persists and is retrievable from observation records"
      - "Observer verification creates observations with reasoning explaining the verification logic"
      - "Deterministic observations created without reasoning have no reasoning field (absent, not null)"
      
      Remove references to internal type names, function names, call chains, and SQL syntax from ACs.
      Move those details to implementation_notes section where they guide implementation without coupling tests.
    
  - id: D2
    severity: BLOCKER
    dimension: "Step Scope & Decomposability"
    location: "roadmap.yaml:38-84 (Step 01-01)"
    description: |
      Step 01-01 threads reasoning through 6 distinct LLM paths in a single step:
      (1) generateVerificationVerdict, (2) generatePeerReviewVerdict, (3) detectContradictions,
      (4) evaluateAnomalies, (5) verifyContradiction, (6) verifyMissingDecision.
      
      While the implementation note claims "not decomposable further because all paths share
      createObservation entry point," this argument conflates schema/query changes (which are truly shared)
      with path-specific logic (which is independent).
      
      A single TDD cycle on this step means: RED phase tests all 6 paths failing, GREEN phase implements
      all 6 paths. This is excessive for one cycle and violates the principle that each step should be
      achievable in a single TDD cycle.
      
      Files modified support decomposition: 9 files = multiple responsibilities (schema, query layer,
      6 observer modules). No single file is modified by all 6 paths equally.
    suggestion: |
      Decompose Step 01-01 into 4 substeps:
      
      01-01: "Schema + query contract + verification path"
        - Files: schema/migrations/*.surql, surreal-schema.surql, observation/queries.ts,
                 llm-reasoning.ts (generateVerificationVerdict)
        - AC: Verification verdicts produce observations with reasoning
      
      01-02: "Peer review path"
        - Files: llm-reasoning.ts (generatePeerReviewVerdict), agents/observer/agent.ts
        - AC: Peer review verdicts produce observations with reasoning
      
      01-03: "Graph scan contradiction and anomaly paths"
        - Files: llm-synthesis.ts (detectContradictions, evaluateAnomalies), graph-scan.ts
        - AC: Graph scan produces observations with reasoning for contradictions and anomalies
      
      01-04: "Trace analyzer paths"
        - Files: trace-response-analyzer.ts
        - AC: Trace analyzer produces observations with reasoning for contradictions and missing decisions
      
      Each substep is independently testable and achievable in one TDD cycle.
      Update dependencies: 02-01 depends on 01-01|01-02|01-03|01-04 (all complete).
      Update 03-01 dependency to require all of 01-*.
    
  - id: D3
    severity: BLOCKER
    dimension: "Test File Mapping"
    location: "roadmap.yaml:209 (Step 04-01)"
    description: |
      Step 04-01 (UI reasoning panel) lists test_file as "tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/observation-reasoning.test.ts",
      which is the Step 01-01 test file for schema and persistence.
      
      Step 04-01 is a UI feature that should test React component rendering, toggle behavior,
      and conditional display based on reasoning field presence. This belongs in a separate test file
      focused on UI behavior.
    suggestion: |
      Create a new test file: tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/view-logic-panel.test.ts
      
      This test file should verify:
      - ReasoningPanel component renders when reasoning field is present
      - Panel is collapsed by default
      - Toggle expands/collapses panel
      - Three states render correctly (available, deterministic, legacy)

summary: |
  The roadmap is REJECTED due to 6 blockers, all stemming from acceptance criteria that are
  implementation-prescriptive rather than behavioral. The core issues are:
  
  1. **Implementation Coupling**: ACs prescribe internal types (VerificationResult, LlmVerdict),
     function names (applyLlmVerdict, persistObservation, generateVerificationVerdict), call chains,
     and SQL syntax (DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE). Tests that depend on these fail when internals
     change, even if behavior is preserved.
  
  2. **Step Decomposition**: Step 01-01 threads reasoning through 6 distinct LLM paths in one step.
     While the note claims "not decomposable," the step actually bundles multiple independent
     responsibilities (schema, query layer, verification path, peer review path, graph scan paths,
     trace analyzer paths). This violates the principle that a step should be achievable in one
     TDD cycle.
  
  3. **Test File Mismatch**: Step 04-01 (UI panel) incorrectly references the Step 01-01 test file.
  
  4. **Over-Specification**: ACs include implementation details (B-tree index, bound parameters,
     specific file locations) that are not observable behaviors.
  
  The acceptance tests themselves (observation-reasoning.test.ts, intent-reasoning.test.ts,
  reasoning-queries.test.ts) demonstrate the correct approach: they verify observable outcomes
  (record fields, data persistence, query filtering) without coupling to internal implementation.
  
  **Action Required Before Approval**:

     Move implementation guidance to implementation_notes.
  
  2. Decompose Step 01-01 into 4 substeps, one per reasoning path or pair of paths.

  3. Create a separate test file for Step 04-01 (UI panel testing).
  
  4. Remove references to internal types, functions, indexes, and SQL syntax from ACs.
     Replace with behavioral language that describes what users/systems can observe.

      - ac_57: "Observer verification path: LlmVerdict.reasoning from generateVerificationVerdict flows through applyLlmVerdict -> VerificationResult -> persistObservation -> createObservation"

        should_be: "Observer verification path produces observations with reasoning attached"

  **Action Required Before Approval**:
  
  1. Rewrite all acceptance criteria to focus on observable outcomes, not implementation.
     Move implementation guidance to implementation_notes.
  
  2. Decompose Step 01-01 into 4 substeps, one per reasoning path or pair of paths.
     Update dependencies accordingly.
  
  3. Create a separate test file for Step 04-01 (UI panel testing).
  
  4. Remove references to internal types, functions, indexes, and SQL syntax from ACs.
     Replace with behavioral language that describes what users/systems can observe.
```

- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/memphis/tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/reasoning-test-kit.ts`

**Verdict: REJECTED** — Roadmap requires significant rework on acceptance criteria before approval.

Roadmap **REJECTED** with 6 blockers. Let me address all issues in a revision:

1. **Rewrite ACs to be behavioral** (remove internal type/function names, SQL syntax)
2. **Decompose Step 01-01** into 4 substeps (one per path group)
3. **Fix test file for Step 04-01**
4. **Move implementation details to implementation_notes**

# LLM Reasoning as Internal Telemetry — Implementation Roadmap
#
# Store LLM chain-of-thought reasoning on Observation and Intent nodes.
# Extends Observer verification pipeline and Intent authorization to persist
# reasoning strings, adds query function for programmatic consumers, and
# gates reasoning fields to admin-only API responses.
#
# Design artifacts:
#   - docs/feature/intent-llm-traces/design/architecture-design.md
#   - docs/adrs/ADR-053-llm-reasoning-as-internal-telemetry.md
#   - docs/requirements/intent-llm-traces/US-01 through US-04
#   - tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/ (37 acceptance scenarios)
#
# Paradigm: Functional (TypeScript)
# Rigor: inherit model, review enabled, mutation disabled

project_id: intent-llm-traces
title: "LLM Reasoning as Internal Telemetry on Intent and Observation Nodes"
paradigm: functional

roadmap:
  project_id: intent-llm-traces
  created_at: "2026-03-16"
  total_steps: 4

validation:
  status: pending

phases:
  - id: "01"
    name: "Observation Reasoning Persistence"
    title: "Schema + Observer Pipeline Threading"
    description: >
      Add reasoning field to observation table, extend createObservation to accept
      reasoning parameter, and thread reasoning from all Observer LLM paths through
      to persistence. This is the critical path unblocking steps 03 and 04.
    steps:
      - id: "01-01"
        step_id: "01-01"
        name: "Schema migration and observation reasoning persistence"
        title: "Schema migration and observation reasoning persistence"
        description: >
          Add `reasoning` field (option<string>) to observation table via schema migration.
          Extend `createObservation()` in observation/queries.ts to accept optional reasoning
          parameter and persist it. Thread reasoning from all Observer LLM paths:
          (1) generateVerificationVerdict -> applyLlmVerdict -> persistObservation -> createObservation,
          (2) generatePeerReviewVerdict -> persistObservation -> createObservation,
          (3) detectContradictions -> runGraphScan contradiction loop -> createObservation,
          (4) evaluateAnomalies -> runGraphScan anomaly loop -> createObservation,
          (5) verifyContradiction -> trace-response-analyzer -> createObservation,
          (6) verifyMissingDecision -> trace-response-analyzer -> createObservation.
          Deterministic observations (source=deterministic_fallback) must NOT have reasoning.
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "observation table has DEFINE FIELD reasoning ON observation TYPE option<string> via schema migration using DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE"
          - "surreal-schema.surql updated with reasoning field definition on observation table"
          - "createObservation() in observation/queries.ts accepts optional reasoning?: string parameter and includes it in the CREATE statement when provided"
          - "Observer verification path: LlmVerdict.reasoning from generateVerificationVerdict flows through applyLlmVerdict -> VerificationResult -> persistObservation -> createObservation"
          - "Observer peer review path: PeerReviewVerdict.reasoning flows through persistObservation -> createObservation"
          - "Graph scan contradiction path: DetectedContradiction.reasoning flows through runGraphScan contradiction loop -> createObservation"
          - "Graph scan anomaly path: AnomalyEvaluation.reasoning flows through runGraphScan anomaly loop -> createObservation"
          - "Trace analyzer paths: verifyContradiction and verifyMissingDecision reasoning flows through their respective createObservation calls"
          - "Deterministic observations (shouldSkipLlm=true) do NOT have reasoning populated"
          - "observation.text (conclusion) and observation.reasoning (chain-of-thought) remain semantically distinct — reasoning is NOT copied into text"
          - "bun migrate applies the migration successfully"
          - "Existing observations without reasoning continue to load (backward compatible)"
        test_file: "tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/observation-reasoning.test.ts"
        files_to_modify:
          - "schema/migrations/NEXT_observation_reasoning.surql"
          - "schema/surreal-schema.surql"
          - "app/src/server/observation/queries.ts"
          - "app/src/server/agents/observer/agent.ts"
          - "app/src/server/observer/verification-pipeline.ts"
          - "app/src/server/observer/graph-scan.ts"
          - "app/src/server/observer/llm-reasoning.ts"
          - "app/src/server/observer/llm-synthesis.ts"
          - "app/src/server/observer/trace-response-analyzer.ts"
        implementation_notes: >
          Check the next migration number by scanning schema/migrations/ for the highest prefix.
          Use DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE for the migration. Wrap in BEGIN TRANSACTION / COMMIT TRANSACTION.
          The VerificationResult type (verification-pipeline.ts) needs a reasoning?: string field added.
          persistObservation (agent.ts) must extract reasoning from VerificationResult and pass to createObservation.
          In graph-scan.ts, the contradiction and anomaly loops already iterate over LLM results — thread .reasoning to createObservation calls.
          In trace-response-analyzer.ts, verify that verifyContradiction and verifyMissingDecision return objects with reasoning fields.
        dependencies: []

  - id: "02"
    name: "Intent Reasoning Persistence"
    title: "Authorizer LLM Chain-of-Thought Capture"
    description: >
      Add llm_reasoning field to intent table, extend LLM evaluator to capture
      full chain-of-thought alongside existing reason summary, and thread through
      evaluateIntent to updateIntentStatus.
    steps:
      - id: "02-01"
        step_id: "02-01"
        name: "Intent LLM reasoning persistence"
        title: "Intent LLM reasoning persistence"
        description: >
          Add `llm_reasoning` field (option<string>) to intent table via schema migration.
          Extend the LLM evaluator Zod schema (evaluationResultSchema in authorizer.ts) to
          include a `reasoning` field for full chain-of-thought alongside existing `reason`
          (one-line summary). Thread the reasoning through evaluateIntent return value to
          updateIntentStatus as `llm_reasoning`. Policy-only evaluations (no LLM call) must
          NOT have llm_reasoning populated.
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "intent table has DEFINE FIELD llm_reasoning ON intent TYPE option<string> via schema migration using DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE"
          - "surreal-schema.surql updated with llm_reasoning field definition on intent table"
          - "evaluationResultSchema in authorizer.ts extended with reasoning field (full chain-of-thought) distinct from existing reason (summary)"
          - "evaluateIntent returns llm_reasoning from LLM evaluator result"
          - "updateIntentStatus in intent-queries.ts accepts and persists llm_reasoning on the intent record"
          - "Policy-only evaluations (evaluation.policy_only=true) do NOT have llm_reasoning populated"
          - "Existing intent.reasoning field (human rationale) is unchanged"
          - "Existing intents without llm_reasoning continue to load (backward compatible)"
          - "bun migrate applies the migration successfully"
        test_file: "tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/intent-reasoning.test.ts"
        files_to_modify:
          - "schema/migrations/NEXT_intent_llm_reasoning.surql"
          - "schema/surreal-schema.surql"
          - "app/src/server/intent/authorizer.ts"
          - "app/src/server/intent/intent-queries.ts"
          - "app/src/server/intent/types.ts"
          - "app/src/server/oauth/intent-submission.ts"
        implementation_notes: >
          The evaluationResultSchema Zod schema needs a new `reasoning` field with .describe()
          instructing the LLM to provide full chain-of-thought reasoning. The existing `reason`
          field should keep its current description (brief summary). StatusUpdateFields type
          in intent-queries.ts needs llm_reasoning?: string added. The intent-submission.ts
          handler must pass evaluation.reasoning through to updateIntentStatus as llm_reasoning.
        dependencies: []

  - id: "03"
    name: "Reasoning Query and API Gating"
    title: "Programmatic Access and Access Control"
    description: >
      Add listObservationsWithReasoning query function for Observer self-calibration
      and behavior scorer. Gate reasoning fields in observation/intent detail API
      responses to admin-role requesters only.
    steps:
      - id: "03-01"
        step_id: "03-01"
        name: "Reasoning query function and API access control"
        title: "Reasoning query function and API access control"
        description: >
          Add listObservationsWithReasoning() query function in observation/queries.ts.
          Returns observations where reasoning IS NOT NONE, filtered by workspace, with
          configurable limit and optional sinceDate. Used by Observer self-calibration
          and behavior scorer (internal server-side only, no new API endpoint).
          Gate reasoning/llm_reasoning fields in observation detail and intent detail
          API responses to admin-role requesters only. Non-admin responses omit the
          fields entirely (not null, per project convention).
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "listObservationsWithReasoning() returns observations with reasoning, confidence, source, evidence_refs, created_at"
          - "Query filters out observations where reasoning is NONE"
          - "Query respects workspace scope via bound parameter"
          - "Query supports configurable limit (default 50) and optional sinceDate parameter"
          - "Query uses existing workspace B-tree index, no KNN"
          - "Results ordered by created_at DESC"
          - "Observation detail API response includes reasoning field for admin-role requesters"
          - "Observation detail API response omits reasoning field for non-admin requesters"
          - "Intent detail API response includes llm_reasoning field for admin-role requesters"
          - "Intent detail API response omits llm_reasoning field for non-admin requesters"
          - "Non-admin responses do not contain null for reasoning fields — fields are omitted entirely"
        test_file: "tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/reasoning-queries.test.ts"
        files_to_modify:
          - "app/src/server/observation/queries.ts"
          - "app/src/server/entities/entity-detail-route.ts"
          - "app/src/server/intent/intent-routes.ts"
        implementation_notes: >
          SurrealQL for the query: SELECT id, text, reasoning, severity, confidence, source,
          observation_type, evidence_refs, created_at FROM observation WHERE workspace = $ws
          AND reasoning IS NOT NONE AND created_at > $since ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT $limit.
          For API gating, check the requester's role at the response serialization layer.
          The observation detail is likely in entity-detail-route.ts — find where entity data
          is returned and conditionally include/exclude the reasoning field based on role.
          For intents, similar gating in intent-routes.ts where intent detail is returned.
        dependencies:
          - "01-01"

  - id: "04"
    name: "UI Reasoning Panel"
    title: "View Logic Collapsible Panel"
    description: >
      Collapsible panel on observation and intent detail views showing LLM reasoning
      with model identifier and trace link. Three states: reasoning available,
      deterministic fallback, legacy empty.
    steps:
      - id: "04-01"
        step_id: "04-01"
        name: "View Logic UI reasoning panel"
        title: "View Logic UI reasoning panel"
        description: >
          Add a collapsible "View Logic" panel to observation detail and intent detail
          views. Panel collapsed by default (reasoning is internal telemetry). Three states:
          (1) Reasoning available — show full text, model identifier from linked trace, trace link.
          (2) Deterministic — show "No LLM reasoning — deterministic observation" message.
          (3) Legacy/empty — show "Reasoning not available" message.
          Model identifier resolved via trace linkage, not stored on observation.
          Panel only renders when API response includes reasoning (admin-only gating from 03-01).
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "View Logic toggle button visible on observation detail for admin users"
          - "Reasoning panel collapsed by default"
          - "Panel shows full reasoning text when reasoning field is present"
          - "Panel shows deterministic fallback message when source is deterministic_fallback and reasoning is absent"
          - "Panel shows legacy empty state message when reasoning is absent and source is not deterministic"
          - "Panel renders in monospace font for reasoning text (code-block style)"
          - "View Logic toggle button visible on intent detail for admin users"
          - "Intent panel shows llm_reasoning text distinct from human reasoning field"
          - "Toggle hides panel on second click"
        test_file: "tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/observation-reasoning.test.ts"
        files_to_modify:
          - "app/src/client/components/observation-detail.tsx"
          - "app/src/client/components/intent-detail.tsx"
          - "app/src/client/components/reasoning-panel.tsx"
        implementation_notes: >
          Create a shared ReasoningPanel component that handles the three states.
          Use shadcn/ui Collapsible component. The panel receives reasoning, source, and
          optional trace info as props. The observation-detail and intent-detail components
          conditionally render ReasoningPanel when reasoning/llm_reasoning is present in
          the API response (which only happens for admins).
          For trace linkage, the API may need to return model identifier from the linked trace
          alongside reasoning — check if entity detail already includes trace info.
        dependencies:
          - "03-01"

# LLM Reasoning as Internal Telemetry — Implementation Roadmap
#
# Store LLM chain-of-thought reasoning on Observation and Intent nodes.
# Extends Observer verification pipeline and Intent authorization to persist
# reasoning strings, adds query function for programmatic consumers, and
# gates reasoning fields to admin-only API responses.
#
# Design artifacts:
#   - docs/feature/intent-llm-traces/design/architecture-design.md
#   - docs/adrs/ADR-053-llm-reasoning-as-internal-telemetry.md
#   - docs/requirements/intent-llm-traces/US-01 through US-04
#   - tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/ (37 acceptance scenarios)
#
# Paradigm: Functional (TypeScript)
# Rigor: inherit model, review enabled, mutation disabled
#
# Revision 2: Addressed all 6 blockers from iteration 1 crafter review.
#   D1: Rewrote all ACs to behavioral outcomes (no internal types, SQL syntax, or call chains)
#   D2: Decomposed Step 01-01 into 4 substeps (01-01 through 01-04)
#   D3: Fixed Step 04-01 test_file to separate UI test file
#   D4: Moved internal type/function references from ACs to implementation_notes
#   D5: Removed file-specific references from Step 02-01 ACs
#   D6: Removed index/parameter implementation details from Step 03-01 ACs

project_id: intent-llm-traces
title: "LLM Reasoning as Internal Telemetry on Intent and Observation Nodes"
paradigm: functional

roadmap:
  project_id: intent-llm-traces
  created_at: "2026-03-16"
  total_steps: 7

validation:
  status: pending

phases:
  - id: "01"
    name: "Observation Reasoning Persistence"
    title: "Schema + Observer Pipeline Threading"
    description: >
      Add reasoning field to observation table, extend observation creation to accept
      reasoning, and thread reasoning from all Observer LLM paths through to persistence.
      Decomposed into 4 substeps: schema+verification, peer review, graph scan, trace analyzer.
      This phase is the critical path unblocking phases 03 and 04.
    steps:
      - id: "01-01"
        step_id: "01-01"
        name: "Schema migration and verification reasoning path"
        title: "Observation reasoning schema and verification path"
        description: >
          Add reasoning field to observation table. Extend observation creation to accept
          and persist reasoning. Thread reasoning from the Observer verification path so
          that verification verdicts produce observations with reasoning attached.
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "Observations can be created with optional reasoning text that persists and is retrievable"
          - "Observations created without reasoning have no reasoning field (absent, not null)"
          - "Observer verification produces observations with reasoning explaining the verification logic"
          - "Deterministic observations (no LLM involved) do not have reasoning"
          - "Observation text (conclusion) and reasoning (chain-of-thought) are distinct values"
          - "Schema migration applies successfully via bun migrate"
          - "Existing observations without reasoning continue to load"
        test_file: "tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/observation-reasoning.test.ts"
        files_to_modify:
          - "schema/migrations/NEXT_observation_reasoning.surql"
          - "schema/surreal-schema.surql"
          - "app/src/server/observation/queries.ts"
          - "app/src/server/observer/llm-reasoning.ts"
          - "app/src/server/observer/verification-pipeline.ts"
          - "app/src/server/agents/observer/agent.ts"
        implementation_notes: >
          Check next migration number by scanning schema/migrations/ for highest prefix.
          Use DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE reasoning ON observation TYPE option<string>.
          Wrap in BEGIN TRANSACTION / COMMIT TRANSACTION.
          Add reasoning?: string to createObservation input type in observation/queries.ts.
          Add reasoning?: string to VerificationResult type in verification-pipeline.ts.
          In llm-reasoning.ts, LlmVerdict already has .reasoning — pass through applyLlmVerdict.
          In agent.ts, persistObservation extracts reasoning from VerificationResult, passes to createObservation.
        dependencies: []

      - id: "01-02"
        step_id: "01-02"
        name: "Peer review reasoning path"
        title: "Peer review observations include reasoning"
        description: >
          Thread reasoning from the Observer peer review path so that peer review
          verdicts produce observations with reasoning attached.
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "Peer review observations include the reviewer's chain-of-thought reasoning"
          - "Peer review reasoning is distinct from the reviewed observation's text"
        test_file: "tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/observation-reasoning.test.ts"
        files_to_modify:
          - "app/src/server/observer/llm-reasoning.ts"
          - "app/src/server/agents/observer/agent.ts"
        implementation_notes: >
          PeerReviewVerdict already has .reasoning field in llm-reasoning.ts.
          Thread it through the peer review observation creation path in agent.ts
          (peerReviewObservation or similar function) to createObservation.
        dependencies:
          - "01-01"

      - id: "01-03"
        step_id: "01-03"
        name: "Graph scan contradiction and anomaly reasoning paths"
        title: "Graph scan observations include reasoning"
        description: >
          Thread reasoning from the graph scan contradiction detection and anomaly
          evaluation paths so that these observations include reasoning.
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "Contradiction observations from graph scan include the detection reasoning"
          - "Anomaly observations from graph scan include the evaluation reasoning"
          - "Filtered anomalies (not relevant) do not produce observations and thus no reasoning is stored"
        test_file: "tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/observation-reasoning.test.ts"
        files_to_modify:
          - "app/src/server/observer/llm-synthesis.ts"
          - "app/src/server/observer/graph-scan.ts"
        implementation_notes: >
          In llm-synthesis.ts, DetectedContradiction and AnomalyEvaluation types have .reasoning fields.
          In graph-scan.ts, the contradiction loop and anomaly loop iterate over LLM results —
          thread .reasoning to createObservation calls within each loop iteration.
        dependencies:
          - "01-01"

      - id: "01-04"
        step_id: "01-04"
        name: "Trace analyzer reasoning paths"
        title: "Trace analyzer observations include reasoning"
        description: >
          Thread reasoning from the trace response analyzer paths (contradiction
          verification and missing decision detection) so that these observations
          include reasoning.
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "Trace contradiction observations include the verification reasoning"
          - "Missing decision observations include the detection reasoning"
        test_file: "tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/observation-reasoning.test.ts"
        files_to_modify:
          - "app/src/server/observer/trace-response-analyzer.ts"
        implementation_notes: >
          In trace-response-analyzer.ts, verifyContradiction and verifyMissingDecision
          return objects with reasoning/summary fields. Thread these to createObservation
          calls within detectContradictions and detectMissingDecisions functions.
        dependencies:
          - "01-01"

  - id: "02"
    name: "Intent Reasoning Persistence"
    title: "Authorizer LLM Chain-of-Thought Capture"
    description: >
      Add llm_reasoning field to intent table, extend LLM evaluator to capture
      full chain-of-thought alongside existing reason summary, and persist on
      intent records.
    steps:
      - id: "02-01"
        step_id: "02-01"
        name: "Intent LLM reasoning persistence"
        title: "Intent LLM reasoning persistence"
        description: >
          Add llm_reasoning field to intent table. Extend LLM evaluator to capture
          full chain-of-thought alongside existing one-line reason summary. Persist
          on intent records when LLM evaluation occurs. Policy-only evaluations
          (no LLM call) must not have llm_reasoning.
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "LLM-evaluated intents include full chain-of-thought reasoning as llm_reasoning"
          - "llm_reasoning is distinct from existing human-provided reasoning field"
          - "llm_reasoning is distinct from evaluation.reason (one-line summary)"
          - "Policy-only evaluations do not have llm_reasoning (field absent, not null)"
          - "Existing human reasoning field is unchanged by this change"
          - "Existing intents without llm_reasoning continue to load"
          - "Schema migration applies successfully via bun migrate"
        test_file: "tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/intent-reasoning.test.ts"
        files_to_modify:
          - "schema/migrations/NEXT_intent_llm_reasoning.surql"
          - "schema/surreal-schema.surql"
          - "app/src/server/intent/authorizer.ts"
          - "app/src/server/intent/intent-queries.ts"
          - "app/src/server/intent/types.ts"
          - "app/src/server/oauth/intent-submission.ts"
        implementation_notes: >
          Use DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE llm_reasoning ON intent TYPE option<string>.
          The evaluationResultSchema Zod schema needs a new reasoning field with .describe()
          instructing the LLM to provide full chain-of-thought. The existing reason field
          keeps its current description (brief summary). StatusUpdateFields type in
          intent-queries.ts needs llm_reasoning?: string. The intent-submission.ts handler
          passes evaluation.reasoning through to updateIntentStatus as llm_reasoning.
        dependencies: []

  - id: "03"
    name: "Reasoning Query and API Gating"
    title: "Programmatic Access and Access Control"
    description: >
      Add query function for observations with reasoning. Gate reasoning fields
      in API responses to admin-role requesters only.
    steps:
      - id: "03-01"
        step_id: "03-01"
        name: "Reasoning query function and API access control"
        title: "Reasoning query function and API access control"
        description: >
          Add query function returning observations that have reasoning, filtered by
          workspace, with configurable limit and optional time range. Gate reasoning
          and llm_reasoning fields in observation and intent detail API responses to
          admin-role requesters only.
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "Query returns only observations that have reasoning attached"
          - "Query is scoped to the requesting workspace"
          - "Query results are ordered by creation date, most recent first"
          - "Query respects configurable limit (default 50)"
          - "Query supports time-range filtering via sinceDate parameter"
          - "Observation detail API includes reasoning for admin-role requesters"
          - "Observation detail API omits reasoning for non-admin requesters"
          - "Intent detail API includes llm_reasoning for admin-role requesters"
          - "Intent detail API omits llm_reasoning for non-admin requesters"
          - "Non-admin responses omit reasoning fields entirely (not null, not empty)"
        test_file: "tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/reasoning-queries.test.ts"
        files_to_modify:
          - "app/src/server/observation/queries.ts"
          - "app/src/server/entities/entity-detail-route.ts"
          - "app/src/server/intent/intent-routes.ts"
        implementation_notes: >
          SurrealQL: SELECT id, text, reasoning, severity, confidence, source,
          observation_type, evidence_refs, created_at FROM observation WHERE workspace = $ws
          AND reasoning IS NOT NONE AND created_at > $since ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT $limit.
          Uses existing workspace B-tree index, no KNN needed.
          For API gating, check requester role at the response serialization layer.
          Observation detail in entity-detail-route.ts — conditionally include/exclude
          reasoning field based on role. Similar gating in intent-routes.ts.
        dependencies:
          - "01-01"

  - id: "04"
    name: "UI Reasoning Panel"
    title: "View Logic Collapsible Panel"
    description: >
      Collapsible panel on observation and intent detail views showing LLM reasoning.
      Three states: reasoning available, deterministic fallback, legacy empty.
    steps:
      - id: "04-01"
        step_id: "04-01"
        name: "View Logic UI reasoning panel"
        title: "View Logic UI reasoning panel"
        description: >
          Add a collapsible View Logic panel to observation and intent detail views.
          Panel collapsed by default. Three states: reasoning available (show full text),
          deterministic (show fallback message), legacy/empty (show not available message).
          Panel only renders when API response includes reasoning (admin-only from step 03-01).
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "View Logic toggle button visible on observation detail for admin users"
          - "Reasoning panel collapsed by default"
          - "Panel shows full reasoning text when reasoning is present"
          - "Panel shows deterministic fallback message when source is deterministic and reasoning absent"
          - "Panel shows legacy empty state when reasoning absent and source is not deterministic"
          - "Reasoning text renders in monospace font"
          - "View Logic toggle visible on intent detail for admin users"
          - "Intent panel shows llm_reasoning distinct from human reasoning"
          - "Toggle hides panel on second click"
        test_file: "tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/view-logic-panel.test.ts"
        files_to_modify:
          - "app/src/client/components/reasoning-panel.tsx"
          - "app/src/client/components/observation-detail.tsx"
          - "app/src/client/components/intent-detail.tsx"
        implementation_notes: >
          Create shared ReasoningPanel component using shadcn/ui Collapsible.
          Panel receives reasoning, source, and optional trace info as props.
          observation-detail and intent-detail conditionally render ReasoningPanel
          when reasoning/llm_reasoning is present in API response.
          Model identifier resolved via trace linkage (observation -> source_session
          -> agent_session -> invoked -> trace for model field).
        dependencies:
          - "03-01"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# LLM Reasoning as Internal Telemetry — Implementation Roadmap
#
# Store LLM chain-of-thought reasoning on Observation and Intent nodes.
# Extends Observer verification pipeline and Intent authorization to persist
# reasoning strings, adds query function for programmatic consumers, and
# gates reasoning fields to admin-only API responses.
#
# Design artifacts:
#   - docs/feature/intent-llm-traces/design/architecture-design.md
#   - docs/adrs/ADR-053-llm-reasoning-as-internal-telemetry.md
#   - docs/requirements/intent-llm-traces/US-01 through US-04
#   - tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/ (37 acceptance scenarios)
#
# Paradigm: Functional (TypeScript)
# Rigor: inherit model, review enabled, mutation disabled

project_id: intent-llm-traces
title: "LLM Reasoning as Internal Telemetry on Intent and Observation Nodes"
paradigm: functional

roadmap:
  project_id: intent-llm-traces
  created_at: "2026-03-16"
  total_steps: 4

validation:
  status: pending

phases:
  - id: "01"
    name: "Observation Reasoning Persistence"
    title: "Schema + Observer Pipeline Threading"
    description: >
      Add reasoning field to observation table, extend createObservation to accept
      reasoning parameter, and thread reasoning from all Observer LLM paths through
      to persistence. This is the critical path unblocking steps 03 and 04.
    steps:
      - id: "01-01"
        step_id: "01-01"
        name: "Schema migration and observation reasoning persistence"
        title: "Schema migration and observation reasoning persistence"
        description: >
          Add `reasoning` field (option<string>) to observation table via schema migration.
          Extend `createObservation()` in observation/queries.ts to accept optional reasoning
          parameter and persist it. Thread reasoning from all Observer LLM paths:
          (1) generateVerificationVerdict -> applyLlmVerdict -> persistObservation -> createObservation,
          (2) generatePeerReviewVerdict -> persistObservation -> createObservation,
          (3) detectContradictions -> runGraphScan contradiction loop -> createObservation,
          (4) evaluateAnomalies -> runGraphScan anomaly loop -> createObservation,
          (5) verifyContradiction -> trace-response-analyzer -> createObservation,
          (6) verifyMissingDecision -> trace-response-analyzer -> createObservation.
          Deterministic observations (source=deterministic_fallback) must NOT have reasoning.
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "observation table has DEFINE FIELD reasoning ON observation TYPE option<string> via schema migration using DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE"
          - "surreal-schema.surql updated with reasoning field definition on observation table"
          - "createObservation() in observation/queries.ts accepts optional reasoning?: string parameter and includes it in the CREATE statement when provided"
          - "Observer verification path: LlmVerdict.reasoning from generateVerificationVerdict flows through applyLlmVerdict -> VerificationResult -> persistObservation -> createObservation"
          - "Observer peer review path: PeerReviewVerdict.reasoning flows through persistObservation -> createObservation"
          - "Graph scan contradiction path: DetectedContradiction.reasoning flows through runGraphScan contradiction loop -> createObservation"
          - "Graph scan anomaly path: AnomalyEvaluation.reasoning flows through runGraphScan anomaly loop -> createObservation"
          - "Trace analyzer paths: verifyContradiction and verifyMissingDecision reasoning flows through their respective createObservation calls"
          - "Deterministic observations (shouldSkipLlm=true) do NOT have reasoning populated"
          - "observation.text (conclusion) and observation.reasoning (chain-of-thought) remain semantically distinct — reasoning is NOT copied into text"
          - "bun migrate applies the migration successfully"
          - "Existing observations without reasoning continue to load (backward compatible)"
        test_file: "tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/observation-reasoning.test.ts"
        files_to_modify:
          - "schema/migrations/NEXT_observation_reasoning.surql"
          - "schema/surreal-schema.surql"
          - "app/src/server/observation/queries.ts"
          - "app/src/server/agents/observer/agent.ts"
          - "app/src/server/observer/verification-pipeline.ts"
          - "app/src/server/observer/graph-scan.ts"
          - "app/src/server/observer/llm-reasoning.ts"
          - "app/src/server/observer/llm-synthesis.ts"
          - "app/src/server/observer/trace-response-analyzer.ts"
        implementation_notes: >
          Check the next migration number by scanning schema/migrations/ for the highest prefix.
          Use DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE for the migration. Wrap in BEGIN TRANSACTION / COMMIT TRANSACTION.
          The VerificationResult type (verification-pipeline.ts) needs a reasoning?: string field added.
          persistObservation (agent.ts) must extract reasoning from VerificationResult and pass to createObservation.
          In graph-scan.ts, the contradiction and anomaly loops already iterate over LLM results — thread .reasoning to createObservation calls.
          In trace-response-analyzer.ts, verify that verifyContradiction and verifyMissingDecision return objects with reasoning fields.
        dependencies: []

  - id: "02"
    name: "Intent Reasoning Persistence"
    title: "Authorizer LLM Chain-of-Thought Capture"
    description: >
      Add llm_reasoning field to intent table, extend LLM evaluator to capture
      full chain-of-thought alongside existing reason summary, and thread through
      evaluateIntent to updateIntentStatus.
    steps:
      - id: "02-01"
        step_id: "02-01"
        name: "Intent LLM reasoning persistence"
        title: "Intent LLM reasoning persistence"
        description: >
          Add `llm_reasoning` field (option<string>) to intent table via schema migration.
          Extend the LLM evaluator Zod schema (evaluationResultSchema in authorizer.ts) to
          include a `reasoning` field for full chain-of-thought alongside existing `reason`
          (one-line summary). Thread the reasoning through evaluateIntent return value to
          updateIntentStatus as `llm_reasoning`. Policy-only evaluations (no LLM call) must
          NOT have llm_reasoning populated.
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "intent table has DEFINE FIELD llm_reasoning ON intent TYPE option<string> via schema migration using DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE"
          - "surreal-schema.surql updated with llm_reasoning field definition on intent table"
          - "evaluationResultSchema in authorizer.ts extended with reasoning field (full chain-of-thought) distinct from existing reason (summary)"
          - "evaluateIntent returns llm_reasoning from LLM evaluator result"
          - "updateIntentStatus in intent-queries.ts accepts and persists llm_reasoning on the intent record"
          - "Policy-only evaluations (evaluation.policy_only=true) do NOT have llm_reasoning populated"
          - "Existing intent.reasoning field (human rationale) is unchanged"
          - "Existing intents without llm_reasoning continue to load (backward compatible)"
          - "bun migrate applies the migration successfully"
        test_file: "tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/intent-reasoning.test.ts"
        files_to_modify:
          - "schema/migrations/NEXT_intent_llm_reasoning.surql"
          - "schema/surreal-schema.surql"
          - "app/src/server/intent/authorizer.ts"
          - "app/src/server/intent/intent-queries.ts"
          - "app/src/server/intent/types.ts"
          - "app/src/server/oauth/intent-submission.ts"
        implementation_notes: >
          The evaluationResultSchema Zod schema needs a new `reasoning` field with .describe()
          instructing the LLM to provide full chain-of-thought reasoning. The existing `reason`
          field should keep its current description (brief summary). StatusUpdateFields type
          in intent-queries.ts needs llm_reasoning?: string added. The intent-submission.ts
          handler must pass evaluation.reasoning through to updateIntentStatus as llm_reasoning.
        dependencies: []

  - id: "03"
    name: "Reasoning Query and API Gating"
    title: "Programmatic Access and Access Control"
    description: >
      Add listObservationsWithReasoning query function for Observer self-calibration
      and behavior scorer. Gate reasoning fields in observation/intent detail API
      responses to admin-role requesters only.
    steps:
      - id: "03-01"
        step_id: "03-01"
        name: "Reasoning query function and API access control"
        title: "Reasoning query function and API access control"
        description: >
          Add listObservationsWithReasoning() query function in observation/queries.ts.
          Returns observations where reasoning IS NOT NONE, filtered by workspace, with
          configurable limit and optional sinceDate. Used by Observer self-calibration
          and behavior scorer (internal server-side only, no new API endpoint).
          Gate reasoning/llm_reasoning fields in observation detail and intent detail
          API responses to admin-role requesters only. Non-admin responses omit the
          fields entirely (not null, per project convention).
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "listObservationsWithReasoning() returns observations with reasoning, confidence, source, evidence_refs, created_at"
          - "Query filters out observations where reasoning is NONE"
          - "Query respects workspace scope via bound parameter"
          - "Query supports configurable limit (default 50) and optional sinceDate parameter"
          - "Query uses existing workspace B-tree index, no KNN"
          - "Results ordered by created_at DESC"
          - "Observation detail API response includes reasoning field for admin-role requesters"
          - "Observation detail API response omits reasoning field for non-admin requesters"
          - "Intent detail API response includes llm_reasoning field for admin-role requesters"
          - "Intent detail API response omits llm_reasoning field for non-admin requesters"
          - "Non-admin responses do not contain null for reasoning fields — fields are omitted entirely"
        test_file: "tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/reasoning-queries.test.ts"
        files_to_modify:
          - "app/src/server/observation/queries.ts"
          - "app/src/server/entities/entity-detail-route.ts"
          - "app/src/server/intent/intent-routes.ts"
        implementation_notes: >
          SurrealQL for the query: SELECT id, text, reasoning, severity, confidence, source,
          observation_type, evidence_refs, created_at FROM observation WHERE workspace = $ws
          AND reasoning IS NOT NONE AND created_at > $since ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT $limit.
          For API gating, check the requester's role at the response serialization layer.
          The observation detail is likely in entity-detail-route.ts — find where entity data
          is returned and conditionally include/exclude the reasoning field based on role.
          For intents, similar gating in intent-routes.ts where intent detail is returned.
        dependencies:
          - "01-01"

  - id: "04"
    name: "UI Reasoning Panel"
    title: "View Logic Collapsible Panel"
    description: >
      Collapsible panel on observation and intent detail views showing LLM reasoning
      with model identifier and trace link. Three states: reasoning available,
      deterministic fallback, legacy empty.
    steps:
      - id: "04-01"
        step_id: "04-01"
        name: "View Logic UI reasoning panel"
        title: "View Logic UI reasoning panel"
        description: >
          Add a collapsible "View Logic" panel to observation detail and intent detail
          views. Panel collapsed by default (reasoning is internal telemetry). Three states:
          (1) Reasoning available — show full text, model identifier from linked trace, trace link.
          (2) Deterministic — show "No LLM reasoning — deterministic observation" message.
          (3) Legacy/empty — show "Reasoning not available" message.
          Model identifier resolved via trace linkage, not stored on observation.
          Panel only renders when API response includes reasoning (admin-only gating from 03-01).
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "View Logic toggle button visible on observation detail for admin users"
          - "Reasoning panel collapsed by default"
          - "Panel shows full reasoning text when reasoning field is present"
          - "Panel shows deterministic fallback message when source is deterministic_fallback and reasoning is absent"
          - "Panel shows legacy empty state message when reasoning is absent and source is not deterministic"
          - "Panel renders in monospace font for reasoning text (code-block style)"
          - "View Logic toggle button visible on intent detail for admin users"
          - "Intent panel shows llm_reasoning text distinct from human reasoning field"
          - "Toggle hides panel on second click"
        test_file: "tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/observation-reasoning.test.ts"
        files_to_modify:
          - "app/src/client/components/observation-detail.tsx"
          - "app/src/client/components/intent-detail.tsx"
          - "app/src/client/components/reasoning-panel.tsx"
        implementation_notes: >
          Create a shared ReasoningPanel component that handles the three states.
          Use shadcn/ui Collapsible component. The panel receives reasoning, source, and
          optional trace info as props. The observation-detail and intent-detail components
          conditionally render ReasoningPanel when reasoning/llm_reasoning is present in
          the API response (which only happens for admins).
          For trace linkage, the API may need to return model identifier from the linked trace
          alongside reasoning — check if entity detail already includes trace info.
        dependencies:
          - "03-01"
