toolUseResult.originalFile:
# LLM Reasoning as Internal Telemetry — Implementation Roadmap
#
# Store LLM chain-of-thought reasoning on Observation and Intent nodes.
# Extends Observer verification pipeline and Intent authorization to persist
# reasoning strings, adds query function for programmatic consumers, and
# gates reasoning fields to admin-only API responses.
#
# Design artifacts:
#   - docs/feature/intent-llm-traces/design/architecture-design.md
#   - docs/adrs/ADR-053-llm-reasoning-as-internal-telemetry.md
#   - docs/requirements/intent-llm-traces/US-01 through US-04
#   - tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/ (37 acceptance scenarios)
#
# Paradigm: Functional (TypeScript)
# Rigor: inherit model, review enabled, mutation disabled
#
# Revision 2: Addressed all 6 blockers from iteration 1 crafter review.
#   D1: Rewrote all ACs to behavioral outcomes (no internal types, SQL syntax, or call chains)
#   D2: Decomposed Step 01-01 into 4 substeps (01-01 through 01-04)
#   D3: Fixed Step 04-01 test_file to separate UI test file
#   D4: Moved internal type/function references from ACs to implementation_notes
#   D5: Removed file-specific references from Step 02-01 ACs
#   D6: Removed index/parameter implementation details from Step 03-01 ACs

project_id: intent-llm-traces
title: "LLM Reasoning as Internal Telemetry on Intent and Observation Nodes"
paradigm: functional

roadmap:
  project_id: intent-llm-traces
  created_at: "2026-03-16"
  total_steps: 7

validation:
  status: pending

phases:
  - id: "01"
    name: "Observation Reasoning Persistence"
    title: "Schema + Observer Pipeline Threading"
    description: >
      Add reasoning field to observation table, extend observation creation to accept
      reasoning, and thread reasoning from all Observer LLM paths through to persistence.
      Decomposed into 4 substeps: schema+verification, peer review, graph scan, trace analyzer.
      This phase is the critical path unblocking phases 03 and 04.
    steps:
      - id: "01-01"
        step_id: "01-01"
        name: "Schema migration and verification reasoning path"
        title: "Observation reasoning schema and verification path"
        description: >
          Add reasoning field to observation table. Extend observation creation to accept
          and persist reasoning. Thread reasoning from the Observer verification path so
          that verification verdicts produce observations with reasoning attached.
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "Observations can be created with optional reasoning text that persists and is retrievable"
          - "Observations created without reasoning have no reasoning field (absent, not null)"
          - "Observer verification produces observations with reasoning explaining the verification logic"
          - "Deterministic observations (no LLM involved) do not have reasoning"
          - "Observation text (conclusion) and reasoning (chain-of-thought) are distinct values"
          - "Schema migration applies successfully via bun migrate"
          - "Existing observations without reasoning continue to load"
        test_file: "tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/observation-reasoning.test.ts"
        files_to_modify:
          - "schema/migrations/NEXT_observation_reasoning.surql"
          - "schema/surreal-schema.surql"
          - "app/src/server/observation/queries.ts"
          - "app/src/server/observer/llm-reasoning.ts"
          - "app/src/server/observer/verification-pipeline.ts"
          - "app/src/server/agents/observer/agent.ts"
        implementation_notes: >
          Check next migration number by scanning schema/migrations/ for highest prefix.
          Use DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE reasoning ON observation TYPE option<string>.
          Wrap in BEGIN TRANSACTION / COMMIT TRANSACTION.
          Add reasoning?: string to createObservation input type in observation/queries.ts.
          Add reasoning?: string to VerificationResult type in verification-pipeline.ts.
          In llm-reasoning.ts, LlmVerdict already has .reasoning — pass through applyLlmVerdict.
          In agent.ts, persistObservation extracts reasoning from VerificationResult, passes to createObservation.
        dependencies: []

      - id: "01-02"
        step_id: "01-02"
        name: "Peer review reasoning path"
        title: "Peer review observations include reasoning"
        description: >
          Thread reasoning from the Observer peer review path so that peer review
          verdicts produce observations with reasoning attached.
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "Peer review observations include the reviewer's chain-of-thought reasoning"
          - "Peer review reasoning is distinct from the reviewed observation's text"
        test_file: "tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/observation-reasoning.test.ts"
        files_to_modify:
          - "app/src/server/observer/llm-reasoning.ts"
          - "app/src/server/agents/observer/agent.ts"
        implementation_notes: >
          PeerReviewVerdict already has .reasoning field in llm-reasoning.ts.
          Thread it through the peer review observation creation path in agent.ts
          (peerReviewObservation or similar function) to createObservation.
        dependencies:
          - "01-01"

      - id: "01-03"
        step_id: "01-03"
        name: "Graph scan contradiction and anomaly reasoning paths"
        title: "Graph scan observations include reasoning"
        description: >
          Thread reasoning from the graph scan contradiction detection and anomaly
          evaluation paths so that these observations include reasoning.
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "Contradiction observations from graph scan include the detection reasoning"
          - "Anomaly observations from graph scan include the evaluation reasoning"
          - "Filtered anomalies (not relevant) do not produce observations and thus no reasoning is stored"
        test_file: "tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/observation-reasoning.test.ts"
        files_to_modify:
          - "app/src/server/observer/llm-synthesis.ts"
          - "app/src/server/observer/graph-scan.ts"
        implementation_notes: >
          In llm-synthesis.ts, DetectedContradiction and AnomalyEvaluation types have .reasoning fields.
          In graph-scan.ts, the contradiction loop and anomaly loop iterate over LLM results —
          thread .reasoning to createObservation calls within each loop iteration.
        dependencies:
          - "01-01"

      - id: "01-04"
        step_id: "01-04"
        name: "Trace analyzer reasoning paths"
        title: "Trace analyzer observations include reasoning"
        description: >
          Thread reasoning from the trace response analyzer paths (contradiction
          verification and missing decision detection) so that these observations
          include reasoning.
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "Trace contradiction observations include the verification reasoning"
          - "Missing decision observations include the detection reasoning"
        test_file: "tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/observation-reasoning.test.ts"
        files_to_modify:
          - "app/src/server/observer/trace-response-analyzer.ts"
        implementation_notes: >
          In trace-response-analyzer.ts, verifyContradiction and verifyMissingDecision
          return objects with reasoning/summary fields. Thread these to createObservation
          calls within detectContradictions and detectMissingDecisions functions.
        dependencies:
          - "01-01"

  - id: "02"
    name: "Intent Reasoning Persistence"
    title: "Authorizer LLM Chain-of-Thought Capture"
    description: >
      Add llm_reasoning field to intent table, extend LLM evaluator to capture
      full chain-of-thought alongside existing reason summary, and persist on
      intent records.
    steps:
      - id: "02-01"
        step_id: "02-01"
        name: "Intent LLM reasoning persistence"
        title: "Intent LLM reasoning persistence"
        description: >
          Add llm_reasoning field to intent table. Extend LLM evaluator to capture
          full chain-of-thought alongside existing one-line reason summary. Persist
          on intent records when LLM evaluation occurs. Policy-only evaluations
          (no LLM call) must not have llm_reasoning.
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "LLM-evaluated intents include full chain-of-thought reasoning as llm_reasoning"
          - "llm_reasoning is distinct from existing human-provided reasoning field"
          - "llm_reasoning is distinct from evaluation.reason (one-line summary)"
          - "Policy-only evaluations do not have llm_reasoning (field absent, not null)"
          - "Existing human reasoning field is unchanged by this change"
          - "Existing intents without llm_reasoning continue to load"
          - "Schema migration applies successfully via bun migrate"
        test_file: "tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/intent-reasoning.test.ts"
        files_to_modify:
          - "schema/migrations/NEXT_intent_llm_reasoning.surql"
          - "schema/surreal-schema.surql"
          - "app/src/server/intent/authorizer.ts"
          - "app/src/server/intent/intent-queries.ts"
          - "app/src/server/intent/types.ts"
          - "app/src/server/oauth/intent-submission.ts"
        implementation_notes: >
          Use DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE llm_reasoning ON intent TYPE option<string>.
          The evaluationResultSchema Zod schema needs a new reasoning field with .describe()
          instructing the LLM to provide full chain-of-thought. The existing reason field
          keeps its current description (brief summary). StatusUpdateFields type in
          intent-queries.ts needs llm_reasoning?: string. The intent-submission.ts handler
          passes evaluation.reasoning through to updateIntentStatus as llm_reasoning.
        dependencies: []

  - id: "03"
    name: "Reasoning Query and API Gating"
    title: "Programmatic Access and Access Control"
    description: >
      Add query function for observations with reasoning. Gate reasoning fields
      in API responses to admin-role requesters only.
    steps:
      - id: "03-01"
        step_id: "03-01"
        name: "Reasoning query function and API access control"
        title: "Reasoning query function and API access control"
        description: >
          Add query function returning observations that have reasoning, filtered by
          workspace, with configurable limit and optional time range. Gate reasoning
          and llm_reasoning fields in observation and intent detail API responses to
          admin-role requesters only.
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "Query returns only observations that have reasoning attached"
          - "Query is scoped to the requesting workspace"
          - "Query results are ordered by creation date, most recent first"
          - "Query respects configurable limit (default 50)"
          - "Query supports time-range filtering via sinceDate parameter"
          - "Observation detail API includes reasoning for admin-role requesters"
          - "Observation detail API omits reasoning for non-admin requesters"
          - "Intent detail API includes llm_reasoning for admin-role requesters"
          - "Intent detail API omits llm_reasoning for non-admin requesters"
          - "Non-admin responses omit reasoning fields entirely (not null, not empty)"
        test_file: "tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/reasoning-queries.test.ts"
        files_to_modify:
          - "app/src/server/observation/queries.ts"
          - "app/src/server/entities/entity-detail-route.ts"
          - "app/src/server/intent/intent-routes.ts"
        implementation_notes: >
          SurrealQL: SELECT id, text, reasoning, severity, confidence, source,
          observation_type, evidence_refs, created_at FROM observation WHERE workspace = $ws
          AND reasoning IS NOT NONE AND created_at > $since ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT $limit.
          Uses existing workspace B-tree index, no KNN needed.
          For API gating, check requester role at the response serialization layer.
          Observation detail in entity-detail-route.ts — conditionally include/exclude
          reasoning field based on role. Similar gating in intent-routes.ts.
        dependencies:
          - "01-01"

  - id: "04"
    name: "UI Reasoning Panel"
    title: "View Logic Collapsible Panel"
    description: >
      Collapsible panel on observation and intent detail views showing LLM reasoning.
      Three states: reasoning available, deterministic fallback, legacy empty.
    steps:
      - id: "04-01"
        step_id: "04-01"
        name: "View Logic UI reasoning panel"
        title: "View Logic UI reasoning panel"
        description: >
          Add a collapsible View Logic panel to observation and intent detail views.
          Panel collapsed by default. Three states: reasoning available (show full text),
          deterministic (show fallback message), legacy/empty (show not available message).
          Panel only renders when API response includes reasoning (admin-only from step 03-01).
        acceptance_criteria:
          - "View Logic toggle button visible on observation detail for admin users"
          - "Reasoning panel collapsed by default"
          - "Panel shows full reasoning text when reasoning is present"
          - "Panel shows deterministic fallback message when source is deterministic and reasoning absent"
          - "Panel shows legacy empty state when reasoning absent and source is not deterministic"
          - "Reasoning text renders in monospace font"
          - "View Logic toggle visible on intent detail for admin users"
          - "Intent panel shows llm_reasoning distinct from human reasoning"
          - "Toggle hides panel on second click"
        test_file: "tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/view-logic-panel.test.ts"
        files_to_modify:
          - "app/src/client/components/reasoning-panel.tsx"
          - "app/src/client/components/observation-detail.tsx"
          - "app/src/client/components/intent-detail.tsx"
        implementation_notes: >
          Create shared ReasoningPanel component using shadcn/ui Collapsible.
          Panel receives reasoning, source, and optional trace info as props.
          observation-detail and intent-detail conditionally render ReasoningPanel
          when reasoning/llm_reasoning is present in API response.
          Model identifier resolved via trace linkage (observation -> source_session
          -> agent_session -> invoked -> trace for model field).
        dependencies:
          - "03-01"

<!-- DES-VALIDATION : required -->
<!-- DES-PROJECT-ID : intent-llm-traces -->
<!-- DES-STEP-ID : 01-01 -->

# DES_METADATA
Step: 01-01
Project: intent-llm-traces
Command: /nw:execute

# AGENT_IDENTITY
Agent: nw-functional-software-crafter

# SKILL_LOADING
Before starting TDD phases, read your skill files for methodology guidance.
Skills path: ~/.claude/skills/nw/nw-functional-software-crafter/
Always load at PREPARE: tdd-methodology.md, quality-framework.md
Load on-demand per phase as specified in your Skill Loading Strategy table.

# TASK_CONTEXT
Step: 01-01 "Observation reasoning schema and verification path"

Description: Add reasoning field to observation table. Extend observation creation to accept
and persist reasoning. Thread reasoning from the Observer verification path so that
verification verdicts produce observations with reasoning attached.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Observations can be created with optional reasoning text that persists and is retrievable
- Observations created without reasoning have no reasoning field (absent, not null)
- Observer verification produces observations with reasoning explaining the verification logic
- Deterministic observations (no LLM involved) do not have reasoning
- Observation text (conclusion) and reasoning (chain-of-thought) are distinct values
- Schema migration applies successfully via bun migrate
- Existing observations without reasoning continue to load

Test file: tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/observation-reasoning.test.ts

Files to modify:
- schema/migrations/NEXT_observation_reasoning.surql (determine next number by scanning schema/migrations/)
- schema/surreal-schema.surql
- app/src/server/observation/queries.ts
- app/src/server/observer/llm-reasoning.ts
- app/src/server/observer/verification-pipeline.ts
- app/src/server/agents/observer/agent.ts

Implementation notes:
- Check next migration number by scanning schema/migrations/ for highest prefix.
- Use DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE reasoning ON observation TYPE option<string>.
- Wrap in BEGIN TRANSACTION / COMMIT TRANSACTION.
- Add reasoning?: string to createObservation input type in observation/queries.ts.
- Add reasoning?: string to VerificationResult type in verification-pipeline.ts.
- In llm-reasoning.ts, LlmVerdict already has .reasoning — pass through applyLlmVerdict.
- In agent.ts, persistObservation extracts reasoning from VerificationResult, passes to createObservation.

PROJECT CONVENTIONS (from CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md):
- Always use --no-verify when committing. Always use -s (GPG sign).
- No null. Use undefined via optional properties (field?: Type).
- Functional paradigm.
- SCHEMAFULL tables only.
- DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE for schema evolution.
- option<string> for optional fields in SurrealDB.
- DEFINE ANALYZER cannot run inside a transaction — place before BEGIN TRANSACTION.
- In DEFINE FIELD statements, FLEXIBLE must come after TYPE.
- Fire-and-forget: Do NOT use void for fire-and-forget DB ops — use deps.inflight.track().
- Run acceptance tests with: bun test tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/observation-reasoning.test.ts

IMPORTANT: Read the existing test file first to understand the test patterns. The acceptance tests
may need adjustment to work with the actual implementation. Focus on making the acceptance tests
pass, not on writing new tests from scratch.

# TDD_PHASES
Execute in order:
0. PREPARE - Load context, read existing files, verify prerequisites
1. RED_ACCEPTANCE - Run the existing acceptance test to see it fail (or adjust it to be runnable)
2. RED_UNIT - Write failing unit test if applicable
3. GREEN - Minimal code to pass tests
   After GREEN: run FULL test suite. If all pass, proceed to COMMIT immediately.
   Never move to new task or stop without committing green code.
4. COMMIT - Stage and commit with conventional message
   Include git trailer: `Step-ID: 01-01` (required for DES verification)
   Example:
   ```
   feat(intent-llm-traces): add observation reasoning schema and verification path

   Step-ID: 01-01
   ```

# QUALITY_GATES
- All tests pass before COMMIT
- No skipped phases without blocked_by reason
- Coverage maintained or improved

# OUTCOME_RECORDING
After ACTUALLY EXECUTING each phase, record via DES CLI:

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/intent-llm-traces \
      --step-id 01-01 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status EXECUTED \
      --data PASS

For SKIPPED phases (genuinely not applicable):

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/intent-llm-traces \
      --step-id 01-01 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status SKIPPED \
      --data "NOT_APPLICABLE: reason"

CLI enforces real UTC timestamps and validates phase names.
Do NOT manually edit execution-log.yaml.

CRITICAL: Only the executing agent calls the CLI.

# BOUNDARY_RULES
- Only modify files listed in step's files_to_modify
- Do not load roadmap.yaml
- Do not modify execution-log.yaml structure (append only)
- NEVER write execution-log entries for phases you did not execute

# TIMEOUT_INSTRUCTION
Target: 30 turns max. If approaching limit, COMMIT current progress.
If GREEN complete (all tests pass), MUST commit before returning — even at turn limit.

# OUTCOME_RECORDING
After ACTUALLY EXECUTING each phase, record via DES CLI:

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/{project-id} \
      --step-id {step-id} \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status EXECUTED \
      --data PASS

For SKIPPED phases (genuinely not applicable):

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/{project-id} \
      --step-id {step-id} \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status SKIPPED \
      --data "NOT_APPLICABLE: reason"

CLI enforces real UTC timestamps and validates phase names.
Do NOT manually edit execution-log.yaml.

CRITICAL: Only the executing agent calls the CLI.
Orchestrator MUST NEVER write phase entries — only the agent that performed the work. A log entry without actual execution is fraud.

# BOUNDARY_RULES
- Only modify files listed in step's files_to_modify
- Do not load roadmap.yaml
- Do not modify execution-log.yaml structure (append only)
- NEVER write execution-log entries for phases you did not execute

# TIMEOUT_INSTRUCTION
Target: 30 turns max. If approaching limit, COMMIT current progress.
If GREEN complete (all tests pass), MUST commit before returning — even at turn limit.
```

**Configuration:**
- subagent_type: extracted agent name
- max_turns by step complexity:

| Step Type | Typical Tool Calls | Recommended max_turns |
|-----------|-------------------|----------------------|
| Hotfix (1 file) | 10-12 | 25 |
| Standard TDD (2-3 files) | 25-30 | 45 |
| Complex (4+ files, new module) | 35-45 | 65 |

Default: 45. Heuristic: `20 + (files_to_modify count * 8)`, capped at 65.

## Error Handling

- Invalid agent: report available agents
- Missing roadmap/execution-log: report path not found
- Step not in roadmap: report available step IDs
- Dependency failure: explain blocking tasks

## Resume vs Restart

When subagent times out:

| Last Completed Phase | Action | Rationale |
|---------------------|--------|-----------|
| GREEN (or later) | Resume | Only COMMIT remains (~5 turns) |
| RED_UNIT with partial GREEN | Resume | Preserves implementation progress |
| PREPARE or RED_ACCEPTANCE | Restart | Little context worth replaying |

Resume costs ~50% more tokens/call due to context replay (measured: 3.7K vs 2.5K tokens/call). For <5 remaining turns, resume is efficient. For 15+ turns, restart with higher max_turns is cheaper.

## Examples

```bash
/nw:execute @nw-software-crafter "des-us007-boundary-rules" "02-01"
/nw:execute @nw-researcher "auth-upgrade" "01-01"
/nw:execute @nw-software-crafter "des-us007" "03-01"  # retry after failure
```

## TDD_PHASES
<!-- Schema v4.0 — canonical source: TDDPhaseValidator.MANDATORY_PHASES -->
<!-- Build system injects mandatory phases from step-tdd-cycle-schema.json -->
{{MANDATORY_PHASES}}

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Agent invoked via Task tool (dispatcher does not execute the work)
- [ ] Step context extracted from roadmap and passed in prompt
- [ ] Agent appended phase events to execution-log.yaml
- [ ] Agent did not load roadmap.yaml

## Next Wave

**Handoff To**: /nw:review for post-execution review
**Deliverables**: Updated execution-log.yaml|implementation artifacts|git commits

<!-- DES-VALIDATION : required -->
<!-- DES-PROJECT-ID : intent-llm-traces -->
<!-- DES-STEP-ID : 01-01 -->

# DES_METADATA
Step: 01-01
Project: intent-llm-traces
Command: /nw:execute

# AGENT_IDENTITY
Agent: nw-functional-software-crafter

# SKILL_LOADING
Before starting TDD phases, read your skill files for methodology guidance.
Skills path: ~/.claude/skills/nw/nw-functional-software-crafter/
Always load at PREPARE: tdd-methodology.md, quality-framework.md
Load on-demand per phase as specified in your Skill Loading Strategy table.

# TASK_CONTEXT
Step: 01-01 "Observation reasoning schema and verification path"

Description: Add reasoning field to observation table. Extend observation creation to accept
and persist reasoning. Thread reasoning from the Observer verification path so that
verification verdicts produce observations with reasoning attached.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Observations can be created with optional reasoning text that persists and is retrievable
- Observations created without reasoning have no reasoning field (absent, not null)
- Observer verification produces observations with reasoning explaining the verification logic
- Deterministic observations (no LLM involved) do not have reasoning
- Observation text (conclusion) and reasoning (chain-of-thought) are distinct values
- Schema migration applies successfully via bun migrate
- Existing observations without reasoning continue to load

Test file: tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/observation-reasoning.test.ts

Files to modify:
- schema/migrations/NEXT_observation_reasoning.surql (determine next number by scanning schema/migrations/)
- schema/surreal-schema.surql
- app/src/server/observation/queries.ts
- app/src/server/observer/llm-reasoning.ts
- app/src/server/observer/verification-pipeline.ts
- app/src/server/agents/observer/agent.ts

Implementation notes:
- Check next migration number by scanning schema/migrations/ for highest prefix.
- Use DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE reasoning ON observation TYPE option<string>.
- Wrap in BEGIN TRANSACTION / COMMIT TRANSACTION.
- Add reasoning?: string to createObservation input type in observation/queries.ts.
- Add reasoning?: string to VerificationResult type in verification-pipeline.ts.
- In llm-reasoning.ts, LlmVerdict already has .reasoning — pass through applyLlmVerdict.
- In agent.ts, persistObservation extracts reasoning from VerificationResult, passes to createObservation.

PROJECT CONVENTIONS (from CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md):
- Always use --no-verify when committing. Always use -s (GPG sign).
- No null. Use undefined via optional properties (field?: Type).
- Functional paradigm.
- SCHEMAFULL tables only.
- DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE for schema evolution.
- option<string> for optional fields in SurrealDB.
- DEFINE ANALYZER cannot run inside a transaction — place before BEGIN TRANSACTION.
- In DEFINE FIELD statements, FLEXIBLE must come after TYPE.
- Run acceptance tests with: bun test tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/observation-reasoning.test.ts

IMPORTANT: Read the existing test file first to understand the test patterns. The acceptance tests
may need adjustment to work with the actual implementation. Focus on making the acceptance tests
pass, not on writing new tests from scratch.

# TDD_PHASES
Execute in order:
0. PREPARE - Load context, read existing files, verify prerequisites
1. RED_ACCEPTANCE - Run the existing acceptance test to see it fail (or adjust it to be runnable)
2. RED_UNIT - Write failing unit test if applicable
3. GREEN - Minimal code to pass tests
   After GREEN: run FULL test suite. If all pass, proceed to COMMIT immediately.
   Never move to new task or stop without committing green code.
4. COMMIT - Stage and commit with conventional message
   Include git trailer: Step-ID: 01-01

# QUALITY_GATES
- All tests pass before COMMIT
- No skipped phases without blocked_by reason
- Coverage maintained or improved

# OUTCOME_RECORDING
After ACTUALLY EXECUTING each phase, record via DES CLI:

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/intent-llm-traces \
      --step-id 01-01 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status EXECUTED \
      --data PASS

For SKIPPED phases:

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/intent-llm-traces \
      --step-id 01-01 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status SKIPPED \
      --data "NOT_APPLICABLE: reason"

# RECORDING_INTEGRITY
Valid skip prefixes: NOT_APPLICABLE, BLOCKED_BY_DEPENDENCY, APPROVED_SKIP, CHECKPOINT_PENDING.
Any other skip reason is invalid and will be flagged.
Anti-fraud rules:
- Only record phases you actually executed
- Do not fabricate test results
- Do not manually edit execution-log.yaml
- A log entry without actual execution is fraud
- SKIPPED phases require a valid prefix and genuine justification

# BOUNDARY_RULES
- Only modify files listed in step's files_to_modify
- Do not load roadmap.yaml
- Do not modify execution-log.yaml structure (append only)
- NEVER write execution-log entries for phases you did not execute

# TIMEOUT_INSTRUCTION
Target: 30 turns max. If approaching limit, COMMIT current progress.
If GREEN complete (all tests pass), MUST commit before returning — even at turn limit.

Description: Add llm_reasoning field to intent table. Extend LLM evaluator to capture
full chain-of-thought alongside existing one-line reason summary. Persist on intent records
when LLM evaluation occurs. Policy-only evaluations must not have llm_reasoning.

Acceptance Criteria:
- LLM-evaluated intents include full chain-of-thought reasoning as llm_reasoning
- llm_reasoning is distinct from existing human-provided reasoning field
- llm_reasoning is distinct from evaluation.reason (one-line summary)
- Policy-only evaluations do not have llm_reasoning (field absent, not null)
- Existing human reasoning field is unchanged
- Existing intents without llm_reasoning continue to load
- Schema migration applies successfully via bun migrate

Test file: tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/intent-reasoning.test.ts

Files to modify:
- schema/migrations/NEXT_intent_llm_reasoning.surql (next number after what exists in schema/migrations/)
- schema/surreal-schema.surql
- app/src/server/intent/authorizer.ts
- app/src/server/intent/intent-queries.ts
- app/src/server/intent/types.ts
- app/src/server/oauth/intent-submission.ts

Implementation notes:
- Use DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE llm_reasoning ON intent TYPE option<string>.
- Wrap in BEGIN TRANSACTION / COMMIT TRANSACTION.
- The evaluationResultSchema Zod schema in authorizer.ts needs a new reasoning field with .describe()
  instructing the LLM to provide full chain-of-thought reasoning. The existing reason field
  keeps its current description (brief summary).
- StatusUpdateFields type in intent-queries.ts needs llm_reasoning?: string.
- The intent-submission.ts handler passes evaluation.reasoning through to updateIntentStatus as llm_reasoning.
- Per project conventions: Do NOT use .optional() in extraction schemas — but this is the evaluator schema, not extraction. Check if evaluationResultSchema follows the same constraint or if optional is OK here.

- Always use --no-verify when committing. Always use -s (GPG sign).
- No null. Use undefined via optional properties (field?: Type).
- Functional paradigm. SCHEMAFULL tables only.

1→# Architecture Design: LLM Reasoning as Internal Telemetry
     2→
     3→**Feature**: Store LLM reasoning on Intent and Observation nodes
     4→**ADR**: [ADR-053](../../../adrs/ADR-053-llm-reasoning-as-internal-telemetry.md)
     5→**Paradigm**: Functional
     6→**Status**: Proposed
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## System Context
    11→
    12→This feature extends two existing subsystems (Observer pipeline, Intent authorization pipeline) to persist LLM chain-of-thought reasoning alongside the records those pipelines already create. No new services, no new external dependencies.
    13→
    14→### C4 Level 1 -- System Context
    15→
    16→```mermaid
    17→C4Context
    18→    title System Context: LLM Reasoning Telemetry
    19→
    20→    Person(admin, "Workspace Admin", "Reviews agent reasoning for forensic debugging and compliance")
    21→    Person(agent, "Coding Agent", "Submits intents via MCP/OAuth")
    22→
    23→    System(brain, "Brain Platform", "Knowledge graph with agent coordination, observation pipeline, and intent authorization")
    24→
    25→    System_Ext(llm, "LLM Provider", "OpenRouter / Ollama -- generates structured verdicts with reasoning")
    26→    System_Ext(surreal, "SurrealDB", "Graph database storing observations, intents, traces")
    27→
    28→    Rel(admin, brain, "Views observation/intent reasoning", "HTTPS")
    29→    Rel(agent, brain, "Submits intents", "OAuth 2.1 + DPoP")
    30→    Rel(brain, llm, "Requests structured verdicts", "HTTPS")
    31→    Rel(brain, surreal, "Persists reasoning fields", "WebSocket RPC")
    32→```
    33→

    74→### C4 Level 3 -- Component: Observer Reasoning Pipeline
    75→
    76→The Observer subsystem has 6 distinct LLM reasoning paths that produce observations. This diagram shows how reasoning flows from each path through to persistence.
    77→
    78→```mermaid
    79→C4Component
    80→    title Component: Observer Reasoning Paths to Persistence
    81→
    82→    Container_Boundary(observer, "Observer Subsystem") {
    83→        Component(verify_verdict, "generateVerificationVerdict", "llm-reasoning.ts", "Semantic entity-vs-decision verification")
    84→        Component(peer_review, "generatePeerReviewVerdict", "llm-reasoning.ts", "Cross-validates observation claims against evidence")
    85→        Component(detect_contra, "detectContradictions", "llm-synthesis.ts", "Decision-task contradiction detection via graph scan")
    86→        Component(eval_anomaly, "evaluateAnomalies", "llm-synthesis.ts", "Stale/drift anomaly relevance filtering")
    87→        Component(trace_contra, "verifyContradiction", "trace-response-analyzer.ts", "Per-trace decision contradiction check")
    88→        Component(trace_missing, "verifyMissingDecision", "trace-response-analyzer.ts", "Unrecorded decision detection in traces")
    89→
    90→        Component(apply_verdict, "applyLlmVerdict", "verification-pipeline.ts", "Merges LLM verdict with deterministic result, preserves reasoning")
    91→        Component(persist_obs, "persistObservation", "agent.ts", "Bridges VerificationResult to createObservation")
    92→        Component(create_obs, "createObservation", "observation/queries.ts", "Writes observation record with optional reasoning field")
    93→
    94→        Component(graph_scan, "runGraphScan", "graph-scan.ts", "Orchestrates graph-wide LLM analysis, pipes reasoning per observation")
    95→    }
    96→
    97→    Rel(verify_verdict, apply_verdict, "Returns LlmVerdict.reasoning")
    98→    Rel(apply_verdict, persist_obs, "Passes reasoning via VerificationResult")
    99→    Rel(persist_obs, create_obs, "Passes reasoning parameter")
   100→    Rel(peer_review, persist_obs, "Returns PeerReviewVerdict.reasoning")
   101→    Rel(detect_contra, graph_scan, "Returns DetectedContradiction[].reasoning")
   102→    Rel(eval_anomaly, graph_scan, "Returns AnomalyEvaluation[].reasoning")
   103→    Rel(graph_scan, create_obs, "Passes per-observation reasoning")
   104→    Rel(trace_contra, create_obs, "Passes ContradictionVerdict.reasoning")
   105→    Rel(trace_missing, create_obs, "Passes missing decision reasoning")
   106→```
   107→
   108→---
   109→
   110→## Component Architecture
   111→
   112→### Observation Reasoning -- Data Flow
   113→
   114→**6 LLM paths** produce reasoning that must reach `createObservation`:
   115→
   116→| # | Path | Source File | Reasoning Origin | Current Observation Creation Site |
   117→|---|------|-----------|-----------------|----------------------------------|
   118→| 1 | Verification verdict | `observer/llm-reasoning.ts` | `LlmVerdict.reasoning` | `agents/observer/agent.ts:persistObservation` |
   119→| 2 | Peer review | `observer/llm-reasoning.ts` | `PeerReviewVerdict.reasoning` | `agents/observer/agent.ts:peerReviewObservation` |
   120→| 3 | Contradiction detection | `observer/llm-synthesis.ts` | `DetectedContradiction.reasoning` | `observer/graph-scan.ts:runGraphScan` (contradiction loop) |
   121→| 4 | Anomaly evaluation | `observer/llm-synthesis.ts` | `AnomalyEvaluation.reasoning` | `observer/graph-scan.ts:runGraphScan` (anomaly loop) |
   122→| 5 | Trace contradiction | `observer/trace-response-analyzer.ts` | `ContradictionVerdict.reasoning` | `observer/trace-response-analyzer.ts:detectContradictions` |
   123→| 6 | Trace missing decision | `observer/trace-response-analyzer.ts` | `verifyMissingDecision.summary` | `observer/trace-response-analyzer.ts:detectMissingDecisions` |
   124→
   125→**Current state**: Paths 1-2 embed reasoning in `VerificationResult.text`. Paths 3-4 embed reasoning in the observation text string via template concatenation. Paths 5-6 embed reasoning directly in the `text` parameter of `createObservation`.
   126→
   127→**Target state**: All 6 paths pass reasoning as a separate `reasoning` parameter to `createObservation`. The `text` field contains the human-readable observation summary. The `reasoning` field contains the raw LLM chain-of-thought.
   128→
   129→### Intent Reasoning -- Data Flow
   130→
   131→**1 LLM path** produces reasoning for intent authorization:
   132→
   133→| # | Path | Source File | Reasoning Origin | Current Persistence Site |
   134→|---|------|-----------|-----------------|-------------------------|
   135→| 1 | Authorization evaluation | `intent/authorizer.ts` | `evaluationResultSchema.reason` | `intent/intent-queries.ts:updateIntentStatus` via `evaluation` object |
   136→
   137→**Current state**: `createLlmEvaluator` returns `{ decision, risk_score, reason }`. The `reason` is a one-line summary constrained by the Zod schema description. The full chain-of-thought is not captured because the schema requests a "brief explanation."
   138→
   139→**Target state**: The LLM evaluator schema is extended to include a `reasoning` field (full chain-of-thought) alongside the existing `reason` (summary). This `reasoning` value flows through `evaluateIntent` -> `updateIntentStatus` as `llm_reasoning` on the intent record. When evaluation is policy-only (no LLM call), `llm_reasoning` is omitted.
   140→
   141→### Schema Changes
   142→
   143→**observation table** -- add field:
   144→- `reasoning`: `option<string>` -- LLM chain-of-thought, omitted for deterministic observations
   145→
   146→**intent table** -- add field:
   147→- `llm_reasoning`: `option<string>` -- Authorizer LLM chain-of-thought, omitted for policy-only evaluations
   148→
   149→Migration: single versioned `.surql` script using `DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE`.
   150→
   151→### Access Control Boundary
   152→
   153→LLM reasoning is internal telemetry. It may contain prompt fragments, entity references, and model-specific artifacts not suitable for all users.
   154→
   155→- **API gating**: Observation detail and intent detail endpoints include `reasoning` / `llm_reasoning` only when the requester has admin role in the workspace
   156→- **UI rendering**: Collapsible panel with three states:
   157→  - **Reasoning available**: Show reasoning text with model identifier (from linked trace) and trace link
   158→  - **Deterministic**: Show "No LLM reasoning -- deterministic observation" message
   159→  - **Legacy/empty**: Show "Reasoning not available for this observation" message
   160→
   161→### Integration with Existing Trace Table
   162→
   163→No model statistics (tokens, cost, latency) are added to observation or intent records. The trace table already captures this per LLM call. Consumers that need model telemetry alongside reasoning follow existing linkage:
   164→
   165→- **Observation -> Trace**: `observation.source_session` -> `agent_session` -> `invoked` -> `trace` (for Observer agent observations), or `observation` -> `observes` -> `trace` (for trace-analyzer observations that link the trace as a related record)
   166→- **Intent -> Trace**: `intent.trace_id` -> `trace` (direct link, always present)
   167→
   168→The UI reasoning panel includes a "View trace" link using these paths.
   169→
   170→### Query Patterns for Programmatic Consumers
   171→
   172→**US-04: listObservationsWithReasoning**
   173→
   174→Workspace-scoped query returning observations where reasoning is present. Used by:
   175→- **Observer self-calibration**: Compares past reasoning against current verdicts to detect drift
   176→- **Behavior scorer**: Evaluates reasoning quality for scoring
   177→
   178→Query shape:
   179→```
   180→SELECT id, text, reasoning, severity, confidence, source, observation_type, created_at
   181→FROM observation
   182→WHERE workspace = $ws
   183→  AND reasoning IS NOT NONE
   184→  AND created_at > $since
   185→ORDER BY created_at DESC
   186→LIMIT $limit
   187→```
   188→
   189→This avoids KNN (no embedding needed) and uses the existing `workspace` B-tree index.
   190→
   191→---
   192→
   193→## Technology Stack
   194→
   195→No new dependencies. All changes use existing technology:
   196→
   197→| Component | Technology | License | Rationale |
   198→|-----------|-----------|---------|-----------|
   199→| Schema migration | SurrealDB `.surql` | BSL 1.1 | Existing migration framework (`bun migrate`) |
   200→| LLM structured output | Vercel AI SDK `generateObject` | Apache 2.0 | Already used in all 6 LLM paths |
   201→| Schema validation | Zod | MIT | Already used for all observer/authorizer schemas |
   202→| UI collapsible panel | React + shadcn/ui | MIT | Existing UI component library |
   203→
   204→---
   205→
   206→## Integration Patterns
   207→
   208→### Observation Creation Contract
   209→
   210→`createObservation` in `observation/queries.ts` gains an optional `reasoning` parameter:
   211→
   212→```
   213→input: {
   214→  ...existing fields...
   215→  reasoning?: string    // NEW: LLM chain-of-thought
   216→}
   217→```
   218→
   219→All 6 LLM paths pipe their reasoning string through their respective call chains to this single persistence function.
   220→
   221→### Intent Status Update Contract
   222→
   223→`updateIntentStatus` in `intent/intent-queries.ts` gains `llm_reasoning` in the `StatusUpdateFields`:
   224→
   225→```
   226→StatusUpdateFields: {
   227→  ...existing fields...
   228→  llm_reasoning?: string    // NEW: Authorizer LLM chain-of-thought
   229→}
   230→```
   231→
   232→The `evaluateIntent` pipeline returns `llm_reasoning` alongside the existing `EvaluationOutput`, and the caller passes it through `updateIntentStatus`.
   233→
   234→### API Response Contract
   235→
   236→Observation detail response (existing `EntityDetailResponse.entity.data`) and intent list/detail responses conditionally include reasoning fields based on requester role. This is a response-level filter, not a query-level filter -- the field is always persisted, only conditionally returned.
   237→
   238→---
   239→
   240→## Quality Attribute Strategies
   241→
   242→### Auditability (Primary)
   243→
   244→- Every LLM-generated observation stores the exact reasoning that produced it
   245→- Reasoning is immutable once persisted (no updates to reasoning field)
   246→- Trace linkage provides model, token count, cost, and latency for the generating call
   247→- Admin-only access prevents reasoning leakage to non-privileged users
   248→
   249→### Testability
   250→
   251→- Each LLM path's reasoning threading is independently testable via unit tests (mock `generateObject`, verify reasoning reaches `createObservation`)
   252→- Acceptance tests verify end-to-end: trigger observer scan -> observation created with reasoning field populated
   253→- Query function `listObservationsWithReasoning` testable with seeded observations
   254→
   255→### Maintainability
   256→
   257→- Single schema migration, two new optional fields
   258→- No changes to observation/intent creation flows beyond adding one parameter
   259→- Backward compatible: existing observations/intents without reasoning continue to work
   260→- `createObservation` contract change is additive (optional field)
   261→
   262→### Time-to-Market
   263→
   264→- Estimated 6.5 days across 4 user stories
   265→- US-01 (observation reasoning) is the critical path -- unblocks US-03 and US-04
   266→- US-02 (intent reasoning) is independent and can be parallelized
   267→
   268→---
   269→
   270→## Deployment Architecture
   271→
   272→No deployment changes. Single Bun server process, single SurrealDB instance. Schema migration applied via `bun migrate` before server restart.
   273→
   274→---
   275→
   276→## Roadmap
   277→
   278→### Step 01: Schema migration + observation reasoning persistence
   279→
   280→**Description**: Add `reasoning` field to observation table. Extend `createObservation` to accept and persist reasoning. Pipe reasoning from all 6 Observer LLM paths.
   281→
   282→**Acceptance Criteria**:
   283→- Observation table has `reasoning` field (option string)
   284→- LLM-generated observations include reasoning from generating verdict
   285→- Deterministic observations omit reasoning field
   286→- Existing observations without reasoning continue to load
   287→- Graph-scan contradiction/anomaly observations include per-item LLM reasoning
   288→
   289→**Architectural Constraints**:
   290→- `createObservation` contract: additive optional parameter only
   291→- Reasoning is the raw LLM string, not a processed/truncated version
   292→- `persistObservation` in `agent.ts` threads reasoning from `VerificationResult` through to `createObservation`
   293→
   294→### Step 02: Intent LLM reasoning persistence
   295→
   296→**Description**: Add `llm_reasoning` field to intent table. Extend LLM evaluator to capture chain-of-thought. Pipe through `evaluateIntent` to `updateIntentStatus`.
   297→
   298→**Acceptance Criteria**:
   299→- Intent table has `llm_reasoning` field (option string)
   300→- LLM-evaluated intents include full chain-of-thought reasoning
   301→- Policy-only evaluations omit `llm_reasoning`
   302→- Existing intents without `llm_reasoning` continue to load
   303→- `llm_reasoning` distinct from existing `evaluation.reason` (summary)
   304→
   305→**Architectural Constraints**:
   306→- `createLlmEvaluator` Zod schema extended with `reasoning` field
   307→- Existing `reason` field kept as-is for backward compatibility
   308→- `StatusUpdateFields` type extended additively
   309→
   310→### Step 03: Reasoning query function and API gating
   311→
   312→**Description**: Add `listObservationsWithReasoning` query. Gate reasoning fields in observation/intent detail API responses to admin role.
   313→
   314→**Acceptance Criteria**:
   315→- Query returns observations with reasoning, filtered by workspace, time range, and limit
   316→- Observation detail API includes reasoning only for admin-role requesters
   317→- Intent detail API includes `llm_reasoning` only for admin-role requesters
   318→- Non-admin responses omit reasoning fields entirely (not null)
   319→
   320→**Architectural Constraints**:
   321→- Query uses existing workspace B-tree index, no KNN
   322→- Role check at API response layer, not query layer
   323→- Omit field from response (per project convention: no null)
   324→
   325→### Step 04: UI reasoning panel
   326→
   327→**Description**: Collapsible panel on observation and intent detail views showing LLM reasoning with model identifier and trace link.
   328→
   329→**Acceptance Criteria**:
   330→- Panel shows reasoning text when available
   331→- Panel shows "deterministic" message when observation has no reasoning and source is deterministic
   332→- Panel shows "not available" for legacy observations
   333→- Model name and trace link displayed from linked trace record
   334→- Panel collapsed by default
   335→
   336→**Architectural Constraints**:
   337→- Model identifier resolved via trace linkage (observation -> trace path), not stored on observation
   338→- Admin-only visibility enforced by API (UI receives no reasoning for non-admins)
   339→

1→# LLM Reasoning as Internal Telemetry — Implementation Roadmap
     2→#
     3→# Store LLM chain-of-thought reasoning on Observation and Intent nodes.
     4→# Extends Observer verification pipeline and Intent authorization to persist
     5→# reasoning strings, adds query function for programmatic consumers, and
     6→# gates reasoning fields to admin-only API responses.
     7→#
     8→# Design artifacts:
     9→#   - docs/feature/intent-llm-traces/design/architecture-design.md
    10→#   - docs/adrs/ADR-053-llm-reasoning-as-internal-telemetry.md
    11→#   - docs/requirements/intent-llm-traces/US-01 through US-04
    12→#   - tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/ (37 acceptance scenarios)
    13→#
    14→# Paradigm: Functional (TypeScript)
    15→# Rigor: inherit model, review enabled, mutation disabled
    16→#
    17→# Revision 2: Addressed all 6 blockers from iteration 1 crafter review.
    18→#   D1: Rewrote all ACs to behavioral outcomes (no internal types, SQL syntax, or call chains)
    19→#   D2: Decomposed Step 01-01 into 4 substeps (01-01 through 01-04)
    20→#   D3: Fixed Step 04-01 test_file to separate UI test file

    23→#   D6: Removed index/parameter implementation details from Step 03-01 ACs

<!-- DES-VALIDATION : required -->
<!-- DES-PROJECT-ID : intent-llm-traces -->
<!-- DES-STEP-ID : 01-01 -->

# DES_METADATA
Step: 01-01
Project: intent-llm-traces
Command: /nw:execute

# AGENT_IDENTITY
Agent: nw-functional-software-crafter

# SKILL_LOADING
Before starting TDD phases, read your skill files for methodology guidance.
Skills path: ~/.claude/skills/nw/nw-functional-software-crafter/
Always load at PREPARE: tdd-methodology.md, quality-framework.md
Load on-demand per phase as specified in your Skill Loading Strategy table.

# TASK_CONTEXT
Step: 01-01 "Observation reasoning schema and verification path"

Description: Add reasoning field to observation table. Extend observation creation to accept
and persist reasoning. Thread reasoning from the Observer verification path so that
verification verdicts produce observations with reasoning attached.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Observations can be created with optional reasoning text that persists and is retrievable
- Observations created without reasoning have no reasoning field (absent, not null)
- Observer verification produces observations with reasoning explaining the verification logic
- Deterministic observations (no LLM involved) do not have reasoning
- Observation text (conclusion) and reasoning (chain-of-thought) are distinct values
- Schema migration applies successfully via bun migrate
- Existing observations without reasoning continue to load

Test file: tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/observation-reasoning.test.ts

Files to modify:
- schema/migrations/NEXT_observation_reasoning.surql (determine next number by scanning schema/migrations/)
- schema/surreal-schema.surql
- app/src/server/observation/queries.ts
- app/src/server/observer/llm-reasoning.ts
- app/src/server/observer/verification-pipeline.ts
- app/src/server/agents/observer/agent.ts

Implementation notes:
- Check next migration number by scanning schema/migrations/ for highest prefix.
- Use DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE reasoning ON observation TYPE option<string>.
- Wrap in BEGIN TRANSACTION / COMMIT TRANSACTION.
- Add reasoning?: string to createObservation input type in observation/queries.ts.
- Add reasoning?: string to VerificationResult type in verification-pipeline.ts.
- In llm-reasoning.ts, LlmVerdict already has .reasoning — pass through applyLlmVerdict.
- In agent.ts, persistObservation extracts reasoning from VerificationResult, passes to createObservation.

PROJECT CONVENTIONS (from CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md):
- Always use --no-verify when committing. Always use -s (GPG sign).
- No null. Use undefined via optional properties (field?: Type).
- Functional paradigm.
- SCHEMAFULL tables only.
- DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE for schema evolution.
- option<string> for optional fields in SurrealDB.
- DEFINE ANALYZER cannot run inside a transaction — place before BEGIN TRANSACTION.
- In DEFINE FIELD statements, FLEXIBLE must come after TYPE.
- Run acceptance tests with: bun test tests/acceptance/intent-llm-traces/observation-reasoning.test.ts

IMPORTANT: Read the existing test file first to understand the test patterns. The acceptance tests
may need adjustment to work with the actual implementation. Focus on making the acceptance tests
pass, not on writing new tests from scratch.

# TDD_PHASES
Execute in order:
0. PREPARE - Load context, read existing files, verify prerequisites
1. RED_ACCEPTANCE - Run the existing acceptance test to see it fail (or adjust it to be runnable)
2. RED_UNIT - Write failing unit test if applicable
3. GREEN - Minimal code to pass tests
   After GREEN: run FULL test suite. If all pass, proceed to COMMIT immediately.
   Never move to new task or stop without committing green code.
4. COMMIT - Stage and commit with conventional message
   Include git trailer: Step-ID: 01-01

# QUALITY_GATES
- All tests pass before COMMIT
- No skipped phases without blocked_by reason
- Coverage maintained or improved

# OUTCOME_RECORDING
After ACTUALLY EXECUTING each phase, record via DES CLI:

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/intent-llm-traces \
      --step-id 01-01 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status EXECUTED \
      --data PASS

For SKIPPED phases:

    PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python -m des.cli.log_phase \
      --project-dir docs/feature/intent-llm-traces \
      --step-id 01-01 \
      --phase {PHASE_NAME} \
      --status SKIPPED \
      --data "NOT_APPLICABLE: reason"

# RECORDING_INTEGRITY
Valid skip prefixes: NOT_APPLICABLE, BLOCKED_BY_DEPENDENCY, APPROVED_SKIP, CHECKPOINT_PENDING.
Any other skip reason is invalid and will be flagged.
Anti-fraud rules:
- Only record phases you actually executed
- Do not fabricate test results
- Do not manually edit execution-log.yaml
- A log entry without actual execution is fraud
- SKIPPED phases require a valid prefix and genuine justification

# BOUNDARY_RULES
- Only modify files listed in step's files_to_modify
- Do not load roadmap.yaml
- Do not modify execution-log.yaml structure (append only)
- NEVER write execution-log entries for phases you did not execute

# TIMEOUT_INSTRUCTION
Target: 30 turns max. If approaching limit, COMMIT current progress.
If GREEN complete (all tests pass), MUST commit before returning — even at turn limit.

data.command:
Review this conversation for items that were discussed but NOT logged via the Brain MCP tools.

IMPORTANT: If this was a trivial session (greetings, test messages, simple Q&A with no implementation work), immediately respond with:
{"decision":"approve","summary":"No substantive work to log"}
Do NOT explain why there is nothing to log.

Otherwise, check each category:

1. **Decisions**: Architecture or design choices where alternatives existed (e.g. choosing a pattern, library, data model, or trade-off). Do NOT log mechanical bugfixes, typo corrections, or changes with only one correct approach. Call create_provisional_decision for each genuine decision.
2. **Questions**: Unresolved questions raised but not logged. Call ask_question for each.
3. **Observations**: Contradictions, duplication, missing items, deprecated patterns, or anomalies noticed. Call log_observation for each.
4. **Task progress**: Tasks worked on whose status wasn't updated. Call update_task_status for each.
5. **Subtasks identified**: Work items or follow-up tasks discussed but not created. Call create_subtask for each (requires a parent task).
6. **Suggestions**: Optimizations, risks, opportunities, missing elements, or potential pivots noticed during work. Call create_suggestion for each with category (optimization|risk|opportunity|conflict|missing|pivot), rationale, and confidence (0-1).

content.content:
1→# FP Principles
     2→
     3→Core functional programming thinking patterns. Language-agnostic.
     4→
     5→Cross-references: [fp-domain-modeling](./fp-domain-modeling.md) | [fp-hexagonal-architecture](./fp-hexagonal-architecture.md) | [fp-algebra-driven-design](./fp-algebra-driven-design.md)
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## 1. Higher-Order Functions as Problem Decomposition
    10→
    11→[STARTER]
    12→
    13→Three operations replace most loops:
    14→
    15→| Operation | Purpose | Replaces |
    16→|-----------|---------|----------|
    17→| **Map** | Transform each element, preserve structure | Loop building new collection |
    18→| **Filter** | Keep elements matching condition | Loop with conditional |
    19→| **Fold** | Accumulate elements into single result | Loop with running total |
    20→
    21→**When to use Map**: Transform every element without changing collection shape. Nested maps handle nested structures.
    22→**When to use Filter**: Select elements without changing their values.
    23→**When to use Fold**: Reduce collection to single value. Accumulator IS your state. Combining function IS your state transition. Folds make state machines explicit.
    24→
    25→**Decision**: "Am I transforming, selecting, or accumulating?" Pick matching operation. If none fit, compose two.
    26→
    27→**Why**: These operations communicate intent. Map says "same shape, different values." Fold says "many inputs, one output." Loops say nothing about intent until you read every line.
    28→
    29→---
    30→
    31→## 2. Type-Driven Design
    32→
    33→[STARTER]
    34→
    35→Write the type signature before implementation. The type tells you what the function can and cannot do.
    36→
    37→**Process**:
    38→1. Declare what the function consumes and produces
    39→2. Ask: "which type-specific operations do I actually use?"
    40→3. Replace concrete types with type variables for everything you don't inspect
    41→4. Add constraints only for capabilities you use (equality, ordering, display)
    42→
    43→**Design progression**: Concrete types -> type variables -> constrained type variables. Each step increases reuse while documenting minimal assumptions.
    44→
    45→**Why**: Function's type signature is a contract. Narrower types mean fewer possible implementations, fewer bugs.
    46→
    47→---
    48→
    49→## 3. Pattern Matching as Decision Decomposition
    50→
    51→[STARTER]
    52→
    53→Decompose decisions by data shape, not boolean conditions. Each clause handles one concrete case. Compiler verifies exhaustiveness.
    54→
    55→**When to use pattern matching**: "What shape is this data?"
    56→**When to use guards/conditions**: "What property does this value have?"
    57→**When to use named bindings**: Intermediate results need a name to avoid repetition.
    58→
    59→**Heuristic**: Prefer small extracted functions over giant match expressions. Pattern match on top-level shape, delegate to named functions for sub-decisions.
    60→
    61→**Exhaustiveness as safety net**: When you add a new variant to a choice type, compiler flags every match that doesn't handle it.
    62→
    63→---
    64→
    65→## 4. Composition Patterns
    66→
    67→[INTERMEDIATE]
    68→
    69→### Partial Application
    70→
    71→Fix some arguments of a general function to create specialized version. Eliminates throwaway helper functions.
    72→
    73→**When**: General function exists and you need specialized version for specific context.
    74→
    75→### Function Composition (Pipelines)
    76→
    77→Chain functions where output of one feeds into next. Each function has single responsibility.
    78→
    79→**Why**: Composition reveals architecture of computation. Pipelines read as sequence of steps, making business process visible.
    80→
    81→### Point-Free Style
    82→
    83→Omit explicit argument when function is just a composition. Use when it reveals intent. Avoid when it obscures meaning.
    84→
    85→---
    86→
    87→## 5. Container Abstractions
    88→
    89→[INTERMEDIATE] -> [ADVANCED]
    90→
    91→Progressive hierarchy for working with values inside containers (nullables, lists, futures, results).
    92→
    93→### [INTERMEDIATE] Transformable Container (Functor)
    94→
    95→**What**: Apply function to values inside container without changing structure.
    96→**Plain English**: "I have a value in a box. Transform the value without opening the box."
    97→**When**: You have nullable/optional/list/future and want to transform contents without inspecting the container.
    98→**Guarantees**: Transforming with identity does nothing. Can fuse or split transformations freely.
    99→
   100→### [INTERMEDIATE] Combinable Containers (Applicative)
   101→
   102→**What**: Apply a function inside a container to values inside other containers.
   103→**Plain English**: "I have a function in a box AND values in boxes. Combine them."
   104→**When**: Validation -- check multiple fields independently, combine results only if all succeed. Doesn't short-circuit; collects all errors.
   105→
   106→### [INTERMEDIATE] Combinable Values (Monoid)
   107→
   108→**What**: Combine two values of same type into one, with default element that changes nothing.
   109→**Plain English**: "I have many values. Smash them together into one."
   110→**When**: Folding/reducing collections. Combining operation must be associative, enabling parallelism.
   111→**Examples**: String concatenation with empty string | addition with zero | list append with empty list.
   112→
   113→### [ADVANCED] Chainable Operations (Monad)
   114→
   115→**What**: Chain operations where each step produces wrapped value, next step depends on previous result.
   116→**Plain English**: "Step 1's output determines what step 2 does. Each step might fail/branch/have effects."
   117→**When**: Sequential dependent operations where each step can fail, branch, or produce effects.
   118→
   119→### Decision Tree: Which Abstraction Do I Need?
   120→
   121→```
   122→Do I need to transform values inside a container?
   123→  YES, one function, one container --> Transformable (Functor)
   124→  YES, combine multiple independent containers --> Combinable Containers (Applicative)
   125→  YES, chain dependent operations sequentially --> Chainable Operations (Monad)
   126→Do I need to combine values of the same type?
   127→  YES --> Combinable Values (Monoid)
   128→```
   129→
   130→### Progression Summary
   131→
   132→Each level adds a new kind of combination:
   133→- **Transformable**: one function, one container
   134→- **Combinable Containers**: one function, multiple containers (independent)
   135→- **Chainable**: sequential dependent operations, each producing container
   136→- **Combinable Values**: same-type values collapsed into one
   137→
   138→### Runnable Example: Map, Filter, Fold on Domain Objects
   139→
   140→```
   141→orders = [Order(100, "pending"), Order(250, "shipped"), Order(50, "pending")]
   142→
   143→pendingTotals = orders
   144→  |> filter (o -> o.status == "pending")    -- [Order(100, "pending"), Order(50, "pending")]
   145→  |> map (o -> o.amount)                    -- [100, 50]
   146→  |> fold 0 (acc, x -> acc + x)            -- 150
   147→```
   148→
   149→---
   150→
   151→## 6. Specialized Chainable Patterns
   152→
   153→[ADVANCED]
   154→
   155→| Pattern | What It Manages | When to Use |
   156→|---------|----------------|-------------|
   157→| **Optional** (Maybe/Option) | Possible absence | Operations that can fail without explanation |
   158→| **Result** (Either) | Failure with context | Operations that fail with error details |
   159→| **Environment** (Reader) | Shared read-only config | Dependency injection, configuration threading |
   160→| **Accumulator** (Writer) | Side-channel output | Logging, auditing, collecting metadata |
   161→| **Stateful** (State) | Sequential state changes | Counters, parsers, accumulators |
   162→
   163→These compose: real applications stack multiple patterns. See [fp-hexagonal-architecture](./fp-hexagonal-architecture.md) for DI patterns.
   164→
   165→---
   166→
   167→## 7. Lazy Evaluation as Design Pattern
   168→
   169→[INTERMEDIATE]
   170→
   171→Separate WHAT to compute from WHEN it gets computed. Define potentially infinite sequences and let consumer determine how much to evaluate.
   172→
   173→**When**: Generating candidates then selecting results | pagination and streaming | decoupling producers from consumers | build systems that only rebuild what changed.
   174→
   175→**Separation principle**: Generate all possibilities, then filter. Declarative style says WHAT you want, not HOW to search.
   176→
   177→---
   178→
   179→## 8. The FP Problem-Solving Method
   180→
   181→[STARTER]
   182→
   183→1. **Start with type signature**: What does this function consume and produce?
   184→2. **Identify traversal pattern**: Map, filter, fold, or search?
   185→3. **Recognize accumulator**: If folding, what is the state and how does each element change it?
   186→4. **Decompose by data shape**: Pattern match on constructors, handle each case independently
   187→5. **Compose small functions**: Build complex behavior from simple, tested pieces
   188→
   189→**Mindset shift**: Describe WHAT to compute (transformations, compositions, constraints) rather than HOW (loops, mutations, control flow).
   190→
   191→| Imperative Thinking | Functional Thinking |
   192→|---------------------|---------------------|
   193→| Loop through items | Map/filter/fold over collections |
   194→| Mutate variables | Transform immutable values |
   195→| Check conditions with if/else | Pattern match on data shapes |
   196→| Inherit from base class | Satisfy capability constraints |
   197→| Call methods on objects | Compose functions into pipelines |
   198→| Handle errors with try/catch | Use Optional/Result for explicit failure in types |
   199→| Pass dependencies explicitly | Use Environment pattern for implicit config |
   200→

1→---
     2→name: tdd-methodology
     3→description: Deep knowledge for Outside-In TDD - double-loop architecture, ATDD integration, port-to-port testing, walking skeletons, and test doubles policy
     4→---
     5→
     6→# Outside-In TDD Methodology
     7→
     8→## Double-Loop TDD Architecture
     9→
    10→Outer loop: ATDD/E2E Tests (customer view) - business requirements, hours-days to green.
    11→Inner loop: Unit Tests (developer view) - technical implementation, minutes to green, RED->GREEN->REFACTOR.
    12→
    13→Outer stays red while inner cycles. Outer drives WHAT to build, inner drives HOW.
    14→Never build components not needed by actual user scenarios.
    15→
    16→## Outside-In vs Inside-Out
    17→
    18→Inside-Out (Classic/bottom-up): discovers collaborators through refactoring. TDD guides design completely.
    19→Outside-In (London/top-down/mockist): knows collaborators upfront, mocks them, implements each moving inward.
    20→
    21→Use Outside-In when: architectural boundaries known (hexagonal), program to interface not implementation.
    22→
    23→## ATDD Integration (Lightweight)
    24→
    25→Original 2008 heavyweight ATDD was "too heavyweight for most real teams." Updated approach (Hendrickson 2024):
    26→- Few Given/When/Then examples, not many | Separate requirements from tests
    27→- Smallest subset of team with relevant skills | Value = shared understanding, not executable specs
    28→- Automate only where high-value
    29→
    30→## BDD Integration
    31→
    32→BDD emerged from Outside-In TDD. Given(context)->When(action)->Then(outcome) maps to outside-in mindset.
    33→BDD reframes TDD as design/specification technique, not just testing. More accessible to stakeholders.
    34→Gherkin: structured format bridging technical/non-technical. Use pragmatically - automate only where high value.
    35→
    36→## Outside-In Development Workflow (Bache)
    37→1. Write Guiding Test (acceptance) from user perspective - thick slice of functionality
    38→2. Start at top-level entry point, design collaborating classes incrementally
    39→3. Use mocks to experiment with interfaces/protocols
    40→4. As each layer implemented, move to previously mocked collaborators, TDD again
    41→5. Never build what isn't needed for actual user scenarios
    42→
    43→## Port-to-Port Testing
    44→
    45→Tests enter through driving port (application service / public API) and assert outcomes at driven port boundaries.
    46→Internal classes (entities, value objects, domain services) exercised indirectly - never instantiated directly in test code.
    47→
    48→Flow: Driving Port -> Application -> Domain -> Driven Port (mocked)
    49→
    50→```python
    51→def test_order_service_processes_payment():
    52→    # Setup - mock driven port (external dependency)
    53→    payment_gateway = MockPaymentGateway()
    54→    order_repo = InMemoryOrderRepository()
    55→
    56→    # Test through driving port (application service)
    57→    order_service = OrderService(payment_gateway, order_repo)
    58→    result = order_service.place_order(customer_id, items)
    59→
    60→    # Assert observable outcomes
    61→    assert result.is_confirmed()
    62→    payment_gateway.verify_charge_called(amount=100.00)
    63→```
    64→
    65→## Unit of Behavior (not Unit of Code)
    66→
    67→Test = story about the problem your code solves. Granularity related to stakeholder needs.
    68→A unit of behavior may span multiple classes. Test from driving port to driven port boundary.
    69→Key question: "Can you explain this test to a stakeholder?" If not, you're testing implementation details.
    70→
    71→## Classical vs Mockist Verification
    72→
    73→Classical TDD: real objects | state verification | less coupled to implementation | survives refactoring better.
    74→Mockist TDD: mocks for objects with behavior | behavior verification | lighter setup | more coupled to impl.
    75→Best practice: combine strategically. Behavior verification at layer boundaries, state verification within layers.
    76→
    77→## Test Doubles Taxonomy (Meszaros)
    78→- Dummy: passed but never used
    79→- Fake: working impl with shortcuts (in-memory DB)
    80→- Stub: predefined answers
    81→- Spy: stub that records interactions
    82→- Mock: pre-programmed with expectations for behavior verification
    83→
    84→Choose type by need: mock for interaction design | stub when don't care about interaction | fake for integration bridge.
    85→
    86→## Hexagonal Architecture Testing Strategy
    87→
    88→### Domain Layer
    89→Tested indirectly through driving port (application service) unit tests with real domain objects.
    90→Domain entities, value objects, domain services are implementation details. Testing them directly couples tests to internal structure.
    91→
    92→Exception: Standalone domain logic with complex algorithms (e.g., pricing engine) MAY be tested directly when complexity warrants it and the class has a stable public interface. This is the EXCEPTION, not the rule.
    93→
    94→### Application Layer
    95→Classical TDD within layer, Mockist TDD at port boundaries.
    96→Use real Order, Money, Customer objects in application service tests.
    97→Mock IPaymentGateway, IEmailService ports when testing orchestration.
    98→
    99→### Infrastructure Layer
   100→Integration tests ONLY - no unit tests for adapters.
   101→Mocking infrastructure inside an adapter test is testing the mock, not the adapter.
   102→Use real infrastructure (testcontainers, in-memory databases) to verify actual behavior.
   103→
   104→### E2E Tests
   105→Minimal mocking - only truly external systems (3rd party APIs beyond your control).
   106→Use real domain services, application services, repositories.
   107→
   108→## Test Doubles Policy
   109→
   110→Acceptable (port boundaries only):
   111→- `Mock<IPaymentGateway>` - external payment service port
   112→- `Mock<IEmailService>` - external email provider port
   113→- `InMemoryUserRepository` - fake for fast tests (implements IUserRepository port)
   114→
   115→Do not mock inside the hexagon:
   116→- Domain entities (Order, Customer) - use real objects
   117→- Value objects (Money, Email) - cheap to create, deterministic
   118→- Application services (OrderProcessor) - use real with mocked ports
   119→- Domain services (PricingService) - use real objects
   120→
   121→## Walking Skeleton Protocol
   122→
   123→At most one walking skeleton per new feature. When `is_walking_skeleton: true` in roadmap:
   124→- Write exactly ONE E2E/acceptance test proving end-to-end wiring
   125→- Implement thinnest possible slice - hardcoded values, minimal branching
   126→- Do NOT write unit tests - the E2E test IS the deliverable
   127→- Do NOT add error handling, edge cases, or validation
   128→- Skip inner TDD loop, go directly from RED_ACCEPTANCE to GREEN
   129→
   130→E2E tests are inherently slow and flaky. The walking skeleton is the ONE justified E2E test per feature.
   131→
   132→## E2E Test Management
   133→
   134→Enable ONE E2E test at a time to prevent commit blocks:
   135→1. All E2E tests except first one marked with skip/ignore
   136→2. Complete first scenario through Outside-In TDD
   137→3. Commit working implementation
   138→4. Enable next E2E test
   139→5. Repeat until all scenarios implemented
   140→
   141→## Step Method Pattern
   142→
   143→Step methods call production services, not test infrastructure:
   144→
   145→```csharp
   146→[When("business action occurs")]
   147→public async Task WhenBusinessActionOccurs()
   148→{
   149→    var service = _serviceProvider.GetRequiredService<IBusinessService>();
   150→    _result = await service.PerformBusinessActionAsync(_testData);
   151→}
   152→```
   153→
   154→Scaffold unimplemented collaborators with `NotImplementedException`:
   155→```csharp
   156→throw new NotImplementedException(
   157→    "Business capability not yet implemented - driven by outside-in TDD"
   158→);
   159→```
   160→
   161→## Business-Focused Testing
   162→
   163→### Unit Test Naming
   164→- Class pattern: `<DrivingPort>Should`
   165→- Method pattern: `<ExpectedOutcome>_When<SpecificBehavior>[_Given<Preconditions>]`
   166→- Example: `AccountServiceShould.IncreaseBalance_WhenDepositMade_GivenSufficientFunds`
   167→
   168→### Behavior Types
   169→- Command behavior: changes system state (Given-When-Then)
   170→- Query behavior: returns state projection (Given-Then)
   171→- Process behavior: orchestrates multiple commands/queries
   172→
   173→### Test Structure
   174→- Arrange: set up business context and test data
   175→- Act: perform business action
   176→- Assert: validate business outcome and state changes
   177→
   178→## Environment-Adaptive Testing
   179→- Local development: in-memory infrastructure for fast feedback (~100ms)
   180→- CI/CD pipeline: production-like infrastructure for integration validation (~2-5s)
   181→- Same scenarios: single source of truth across all environments
   182→

data.command:
Review this conversation for items that were discussed but NOT logged via the Brain MCP tools.

IMPORTANT: If this was a trivial session (greetings, test messages, simple Q&A with no implementation work), immediately respond with:
{"decision":"approve","summary":"No substantive work to log"}
Do NOT explain why there is nothing to log.

Otherwise, check each category:

1. **Decisions**: Architecture or design choices where alternatives existed (e.g. choosing a pattern, library, data model, or trade-off). Do NOT log mechanical bugfixes, typo corrections, or changes with only one correct approach. Call create_provisional_decision for each genuine decision.
2. **Questions**: Unresolved questions raised but not logged. Call ask_question for each.
3. **Observations**: Contradictions, duplication, missing items, deprecated patterns, or anomalies noticed. Call log_observation for each.
4. **Task progress**: Tasks worked on whose status wasn't updated. Call update_task_status for each.
5. **Subtasks identified**: Work items or follow-up tasks discussed but not created. Call create_subtask for each (requires a parent task).
6. **Suggestions**: Optimizations, risks, opportunities, missing elements, or potential pivots noticed during work. Call create_suggestion for each with category (optimization|risk|opportunity|conflict|missing|pivot), rationale, and confidence (0-1).

After logging everything, respond with valid JSON in this exact shape:
{"decision":"approve","summary":"<one-line session summary>","decisions_made":["<decision-id>"],"questions_asked":["<question-id>"],"tasks_progressed":[{"task_id":"<task-id>","from_status":"<from>","to_status":"<to>"}],"files_changed":[{"path":"<path>","change_type":"created|modified|deleted"}],"observations_logged":["<observation-id>"],"subtasks_created":["<subtask-id>"],"suggestions_created":["<suggestion-id>"]}

If you cannot log required items (for example MCP unavailable), respond with:
{"decision":"block","reason":"Log these items first: ..."}

Typecheck clean, 1165 unit tests pass, 12 acceptance tests pass.
